The 'condition' string uses ServiceNow's GlideRecord encoded-query syntax: type=General Inquiry^EQ means 'type field exactly equals General Inquiry' (the ^EQ suffix is the equality operator marker - some platform releases serialize it as '=' alone, others append ^EQ). The filter_status_changes_only flag is the platform-canonical way to express 'fire only when the status field changes value' which is more selective than firing on every update." }, "inputs": [], "outputs": [], "stages": [], "actions": [ { "id": "step_if_status_open", "order": 100, "name": "If status is Open", "type": "if", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Open' (Title Case, single word). On match, dispatches to the validate_open_transition subflow which validates that assigned_group is populated. The condition uses the new value of the field (current.status), not the previous value (previous.status), because the trigger has already established that status changed and we are interested in the destination state.", "condition": "current.status == 'Open'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_open", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_open_transition subflow which checks that assigned_group is populated before allowing Draft -> Open. The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToOpen and throws a blocking form-level error if the precondition fails. Subflow lookup is by NAME (not by sys_id) per AAP Section 0.7.2 No-Hardcoded-Sys_ID constraint.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_inprogress", "order": 200, "name": "Else if status is In Progress", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'In Progress' (Title Case, two words separated by single ASCII space). On match, dispatches to the validate_inprogress_transition subflow which validates that assigned_agent is populated AND is a member of assigned_group, and clears pending_reason if the source state was Pending.", "condition": "current.status == 'In Progress'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_inprogress", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_inprogress_transition subflow which checks that assigned_agent is populated AND is a member of assigned_group before allowing Open -> In Progress (or Pending -> In Progress). The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToInProgress and throws a blocking form-level error if the compound precondition fails. Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_pending", "order": 300, "name": "Else if status is Pending", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Pending'. On match, dispatches to the validate_pending_transition subflow which has no precondition gate but DOES set the pending_reason field (Awaiting Info / Awaiting Third Party / Other) per AAP Section 0.5.5.", "condition": "current.status == 'Pending'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_pending", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_pending_transition subflow which stamps pending_reason on the case row. No precondition gate; the In Progress -> Pending transition is unconditionally allowed per AAP Section 0.5.5. The pending_reason value is captured from the calling user's prompt input and persisted via Update Record action inside the subflow. Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_resolved", "order": 400, "name": "Else if status is Resolved", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Resolved'. On match, dispatches to the validate_resolved_transition subflow which queries child x_casemgmt_case_task records via GlideAggregate and surfaces the VERBATIM AAP Section 0.7.4 error message 'All tasks must be closed before resolving this case.' if any open child tasks exist.", "condition": "current.status == 'Resolved'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_resolved", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_resolved_transition subflow which enforces the all-tasks-closed gate before allowing In Progress -> Resolved. The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToResolved (which delegates to GlideAggregate('x_casemgmt_case_task') with addQuery('case', caseSysId) and addQuery('status', '!=', 'Closed')). Throws the VERBATIM AAP Section 0.7.4 error message on failure. Replaces ArkCase CaseFileTasksService (which used Solr search; Solr is out of POC scope per AAP Section 0.3.2). Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_closed", "order": 500, "name": "Else if status is Closed", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Closed'. On match, dispatches to the validate_closed_transition subflow which enforces the case_manager role gate before allowing Resolved -> Closed and auto-sets the closed_date field.", "condition": "current.status == 'Closed'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_closed", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_closed_transition subflow which (a) checks gs.getUser().hasRole('x_casemgmt_case_manager') and (b) auto-sets closed_date = gs.nowDateTime() per AAP Section 0.5.5. The role check evaluates against the saving user because this parent flow's run_as is user_who_triggers (NOT system_user, which would bypass the role gate). Subflow lookup is by NAME and the role lookup inside the subflow is also by NAME (x_casemgmt_case_manager) per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] } ], "tables_referenced": [ "x_casemgmt_case", "x_casemgmt_case_task" ], "subflows_called": [ "x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition" ], "script_includes_used": [ "x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator" ] }]]></master_snapshot> <master_snapshot_id/> <name>x_casemgmt_general_inquiry_state_machine</name> <natlang/> <nlu_model_version/> <outputs/> <protection_policy/> <remote_published_version/> <run_as>user_who_triggers</run_as> <sc_callable>false</sc_callable> <show_draft_actions>false</show_draft_actions> <show_triggered_flows>false</show_triggered_flows> <status>published</status> <sys_class_name>sys_hub_flow</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>86ba682d60283964e395a20554c7d16c</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>x_casemgmt_general_inquiry_state_machine</sys_name> <sys_overrides/> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>sys_hub_flow_x_casemgmt_general_inquiry_state_machine</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <type>flow</type> <validation_status>complete</validation_status> </sys_hub_flow> </record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: Complaint State Machine
sys_update_xml.payload: <record_update table="sys_hub_flow"> <sys_hub_flow action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <access>public</access> <acls/> <active>true</active> <annotation/> <callable_by_client_api>false</callable_by_client_api> <category display_value="Custom">custom</category> <copied_from/> <copied_from_name/> <description>Parent Flow Designer flow that orchestrates every status transition for cases of type Complaint on x_casemgmt_case. OnUpdate trigger filtered on type=Complaint AND status change. Dispatches to validate_open_transition, validate_inprogress_transition, validate_pending_transition, validate_resolved_transition, or validate_closed_transition subflow under ./sub_flows/ based on the new status value via a 5-branch If/Else-If ladder. Replaces the ArkCase ChangeCaseFileStateService + ChangeCaseStatusWorkflowListener + CaseFileQueueHandler stack (the BPMN/Activiti engine is eliminated entirely per AAP Section 0.1.2). Runs as user_who_triggers so that the role-based gate in validate_closed_transition (gs.getUser().hasRole('x_casemgmt_case_manager')) evaluates against the saving user rather than the system user - non-negotiable per AAP Section 0.7.4. All transition rules and verbatim error messages are sourced from AAP Sections 0.5.5 and 0.7.4 and surfaced from the dispatched subflows or supporting business rules (../business_rules/x_casemgmt_block_draft_backtransition.xml and ../business_rules/x_casemgmt_block_terminal_closed.xml). NO hardcoded sys_ids in any reference field - the trigger's table reference, every subflow call, and every scope reference resolve by name (table=x_casemgmt_case, subflow_name=x_casemgmt_validate_*_transition, scope=x_casemgmt). Active=true and status=published per AAP Section 0.7.3 Gate 7 so Update Set commit immediately enables the state machine. Functionally identical to ./general_inquiry_state_machine.xml except the trigger filter is type=Complaint rather than type=General Inquiry - the duplication is intentional per AAP Section 0.4.3 to keep per-type trigger filtering explicit and to allow future per-type customization. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint and zero hardcoded sys_id reference literals.</description> <internal_name>x_casemgmt_complaint_state_machine</internal_name> <label_cache>Complaint State Machine</label_cache> <latest_snapshot><![CDATA[{ "name": "x_casemgmt_complaint_state_machine", "label": "Complaint State Machine", "type": "flow", "description": "Parent state-machine flow for cases of type=Complaint. On match, dispatches to the validate_inprogress_transition subflow which validates that assigned_agent is populated AND is a member of assigned_group, and clears pending_reason if the source state was Pending.", "condition": "current.status == 'In Progress'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_inprogress", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_inprogress_transition subflow which checks that assigned_agent is populated AND is a member of assigned_group before allowing Open -> In Progress (or Pending -> In Progress). The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToInProgress and throws a blocking form-level error if the compound precondition fails. Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_pending", "order": 300, "name": "Else if status is Pending", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Pending'. On match, dispatches to the validate_pending_transition subflow which has no precondition gate but DOES set the pending_reason field (Awaiting Info / Awaiting Third Party / Other) per AAP Section 0.5.5.", "condition": "current.status == 'Pending'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_pending", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_pending_transition subflow which stamps pending_reason on the case row. No precondition gate; the In Progress -> Pending transition is unconditionally allowed per AAP Section 0.5.5. The pending_reason value is captured from the calling user's prompt input and persisted via Update Record action inside the subflow. Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_resolved", "order": 400, "name": "Else if status is Resolved", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Resolved'. On match, dispatches to the validate_resolved_transition subflow which queries child x_casemgmt_case_task records via GlideAggregate and surfaces the VERBATIM AAP Section 0.7.4 error message 'All tasks must be closed before resolving this case.' if any open child tasks exist.", "condition": "current.status == 'Resolved'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_resolved", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_resolved_transition subflow which enforces the all-tasks-closed gate before allowing In Progress -> Resolved. The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToResolved (which delegates to GlideAggregate('x_casemgmt_case_task') with addQuery('case', caseSysId) and addQuery('status', '!=', 'Closed')). Throws the VERBATIM AAP Section 0.7.4 error message on failure. Replaces ArkCase CaseFileTasksService (which used Solr search; Solr is out of POC scope per AAP Section 0.3.2). Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_closed", "order": 500, "name": "Else if status is Closed", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Closed'. On match, dispatches to the validate_closed_transition subflow which enforces the case_manager role gate before allowing Resolved -> Closed and auto-sets the closed_date field.", "condition": "current.status == 'Closed'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_closed", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_closed_transition subflow which (a) checks gs.getUser().hasRole('x_casemgmt_case_manager') and (b) auto-sets closed_date = gs.nowDateTime() per AAP Section 0.5.5. The role check evaluates against the saving user because this parent flow's run_as is user_who_triggers (NOT system_user, which would bypass the role gate). Subflow lookup is by NAME and the role lookup inside the subflow is also by NAME (x_casemgmt_case_manager) per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] } ], "tables_referenced": [ "x_casemgmt_case", "x_casemgmt_case_task" ], "subflows_called": [ "x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition" ], "script_includes_used": [ "x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator" ] }]]></master_snapshot> <master_snapshot_id/> <name>x_casemgmt_complaint_state_machine</name> <natlang/> <nlu_model_version/> <outputs/> <protection_policy/> <remote_published_version/> <run_as>user_who_triggers</run_as> <sc_callable>false</sc_callable> <show_draft_actions>false</show_draft_actions> <show_triggered_flows>false</show_triggered_flows> <status>published</status> <sys_class_name>sys_hub_flow</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>789e559c36dbf6890bb32580a1553e54</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>x_casemgmt_complaint_state_machine</sys_name> <sys_overrides/> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>sys_hub_flow_x_casemgmt_complaint_state_machine</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <type>flow</type> <validation_status>complete</validation_status> </sys_hub_flow> </record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_block_draft_backtransition
sys_update_xml.payload:
<record_update table="sys_script">
    <sys_script action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE">
        <abort_action>false</abort_action>
        <access>package_private</access>
        <action_delete>false</action_delete>
        <action_insert>false</action_insert>
        <action_query>false</action_query>
        <action_update>true</action_update>
        <active>true</active>
        <add_message>false</add_message>
        <advanced>true</advanced>
        <change_fields>false</change_fields>
        <client_callable>false</client_callable>
        <collection>x_casemgmt_case</collection>
        <condition/>
        <description>Before-update guard on x_casemgmt_case: blocks any transition from a non-Draft status back to Draft. When current.status === 'Draft' AND previous.status !== 'Draft' AND previous.status !== '', delegates to x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.validateNoBacktransition(previousStatus, newStatus) and surfaces the verbatim error message 'Cases cannot be returned to Draft.' via gs.addErrorMessage() + current.setAbortAction(true) when the validator returns {ok: false}. Per AAP Sections 0.5.5 (transition matrix row 7: 'Any -> Draft: PROHIBITED') and 0.7.4 (verbatim error message - non-negotiable). Order=200 is the SECOND-HIGHEST priority before-update rule in this scoped application; runs AFTER block_terminal_closed (order=100) so terminal-Closed cases short-circuit before this guard, and BEFORE all subsequent housekeeping rules (validate_assigned_agent_membership at 300, clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress at 400, set_closed_date at 500). The Script Include is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the verbatim error message; this BR contains zero inline error strings. action_insert=false because Draft is the legitimate initial status (per dictionary default_value); only the BACK-transition from a non-Draft state is forbidden. Defensive previous-null guard handles async/system contexts where previous can be null. Skip-if-no-change guard (prevStatus === newStatus) avoids unnecessary Script Include instantiation on non-status-changing updates. Type-agnostic: fires for both General Inquiry and Complaint case types. Defense in depth: the per-type Flow Designer flows do NOT enforce this rule, so this BR is the only enforcer for direct DB modifications via Table API, scripted REST, GlideRecord scripts, and bulk imports. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint and zero hardcoded sys_id literals.</description>
        <execute_function>false</execute_function>
        <filter_condition/>
        <is_rest>false</is_rest>
        <message/>
        <name>x_casemgmt_block_draft_backtransition</name>
        <order>200</order>
        <priority>100</priority>
        <rest_method/>
        <rest_method_text/>
        <rest_service/>
        <rest_service_text/>
        <rest_variables/>
        <role_conditions/>
        <script><![CDATA[(function executeRule(current, previous /*null when async*/) {
    // Per AAP Section 0.5.5 (transition matrix row 7):
    //     "Any -> Draft: PROHIBITED"
    //     Failure surface: "Cases cannot be returned to Draft."
    //
    // Per AAP Section 0.7.4: the literal text "Cases cannot be returned to
    // Draft." is the VERBATIM, NON-NEGOTIABLE error message surfaced when
    // this guard fires. Character-for-character: capital "C", capital "D",
    // trailing period, exact wording. The message lives ONCE in the Script
    // Include's validateNoBacktransition() return; this BR reads it from
    // result.error and never inlines it.
    //
    // Order=200 makes this the SECOND before-update rule in this scoped
    // application. Order=100 (block_terminal_closed) runs first to catch
    // the more aggressive Closed-terminal prohibition; once that rule has
    // confirmed the case is not Closed, this rule fires at order=200 to
    // catch the more specific Any->Draft case. Subsequent rules
    // (validate_assigned_agent_membership at 300,
    // clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress at 400, set_closed_date at 500)
    // are short-circuited when this rule aborts via setAbortAction(true).
    //
    // Delegates to x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.validateNoBacktransition()
    // which returns { ok: true } when the transition is allowed, or
    // { ok: false, error: 'Cases cannot be returned to Draft.' } when
    // transitioning TO Draft from a non-Draft state (prev !== '' &&
    // prev !== 'Draft' && next === 'Draft'). The Script Include is the
    // single source of truth for the verbatim message and the prohibited
    // -transition matrix; this BR consumes its return shape and surfaces
    // the error.
    //
    // ArkCase source-side analog: ChangeCaseFileStateService at
    // acm-plugins/acm-default-plugins/acm-case-file-plugin/src/main/java/com/
    // armedia/acm/plugins/casefile/service/ChangeCaseFileStateService.java
    // handled state changes via changeCaseState() (line 95: setStatus)
    // without enforcing a Draft-backtransition rule. Backtransition behavior
    // was implicit in Activiti BPMN process definitions and queue handlers
    // (e.g., CaseFileQueueHandler.java set status to ACTIVE pre-save). This
    // BR makes the rule EXPLICIT at the data layer, hardening the
    // state-machine contract per AAP Section 0.5.5.

    // Defensive previous-null guard: the `previous` GlideRecord is null in
    // certain async/system contexts (scheduled jobs invoking
    // GlideRecord.update(), some bulk import scenarios, server-side script
    // paths that call GlideRecord.updateMultiple()). If there is no previous
    // state, no transition can be detected, and the rule does nothing.
    if (!previous) {
        return;
    }

    // String() cast normalizes the GlideElement to a primitive string for
    // safe === comparison; without the cast, GlideElement-vs-string equality
    // can behave inconsistently across releases. The Title Case literal
    // 'Draft' (5 chars, single word) is the verbatim choice value from
    // ../choices/sys_choice_case_status.xml per AAP Section 0.5.7. Variants
    // like 'DRAFT' (uppercase, ArkCase Java legacy from CaseFile.java
    // @PrePersist hook), 'draft' (lowercase), or 'Drafting' (extended)
    // would silently never match.
    var prevStatus = String(previous.status);
    var newStatus = String(current.status);

    // Skip if status didn't change - this rule only cares about transitions.
    // Avoids unnecessary Script Include instantiation on the vast majority
    // of UPDATE operations (those that don't change the status field).
    if (prevStatus === newStatus) {
        return;
    }

    // Only fire when transitioning TO Draft from a non-Draft non-empty state.
    // Three conditions, all required:
    //   (1) newStatus === 'Draft'           - we're transitioning toward Draft
    //   (2) prevStatus !== 'Draft'          - we're moving FROM a non-Draft state
    //   (3) prevStatus !== ''               - defensive: empty previousStatus
    //                                         on a brand-new record can't reach
    //                                         this BR because action_insert is
    //                                         false; this guard is belt-and-
    //                                         suspenders for unusual contexts
    //                                         where the platform might emit an
    //                                         empty-string previous.status on a
    //                                         pseudo-update.
    // This BR's pre-check is redundant with the Script Include's authoritative
    // matrix but acts as a fast-path filter to avoid Script Include
    // instantiation cost on the vast majority of updates. Defense in depth:
    // if this pre-check were ever loosened, the Script Include's matrix
    // would still produce the correct result.
    if (newStatus === 'Draft' && prevStatus !== 'Draft' && prevStatus !== '') {
        // Resolve the Script Include via the standard scoped reference syntax.
        // The `x_casemgmt` portion is the scope's vendor_prefix from
        // ../app/sys_app/x_casemgmt_case_management.xml; the
        // `CaseTransitionValidator` portion is the api_name of the Script
        // Include. NO sys_id literal - the resolution is by scoped class
        // name only.
        var validator = new x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator();
        var result = validator.validateNoBacktransition(prevStatus, newStatus);
        if (!result.ok) {
            // gs.addErrorMessage(result.error) surfaces the verbatim message
            // at the top of the form when the user attempts the save through
            // the UI, and is captured in the API response when the update
            // arrives via Table API or scripted REST. The string is read from
            // result.error - never inlined here - so the verbatim wording
            // contract is enforced by the Script Include alone.
            gs.addErrorMessage(result.error);
            // current.setAbortAction(true) cancels the database write. Both
            // the gs.addErrorMessage() and setAbortAction(true) calls are
            // required: the former produces a visible error to the caller,
            // the latter prevents the write from being persisted. Without
            // setAbortAction(true), the message would appear but the row
            // would still update; without gs.addErrorMessage(), the write
            // would be silently rejected with no visible failure.
            current.setAbortAction(true);
        }
    }
})(current, previous);
]]></script>
        <sys_class_name>sys_script</sys_class_name>
        <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by>
        <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on>
        <sys_domain>global</sys_domain>
        <sys_domain_path>/</sys_domain_path>
        <sys_id>6396a298689f7e5a5b41ff6afcece502</sys_id>
        <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count>
        <sys_name>x_casemgmt_block_draft_backtransition</sys_name>
        <sys_overrides/>
        <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package>
        <sys_policy/>
        <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope>
        <sys_update_name>sys_script_x_casemgmt_block_draft_backtransition</sys_update_name>
        <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by>
        <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on>
        <template/>
        <when>before</when>
    </sys_script>
</record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_block_terminal_closed
sys_update_xml.payload:
<record_update table="sys_script">
    <sys_script action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE">
        <abort_action>false</abort_action>
        <access>package_private</access>
        <action_delete>false</action_delete>
        <action_insert>false</action_insert>
        <action_query>false</action_query>
        <action_update>true</action_update>
        <active>true</active>
        <add_message>false</add_message>
        <advanced>true</advanced>
        <change_fields>false</change_fields>
        <client_callable>false</client_callable>
        <collection>x_casemgmt_case</collection>
        <condition/>
        <description>Before-update guard on x_casemgmt_case: blocks ANY transition out of the Closed terminal status. When previous.status === 'Closed', delegates to x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.validateNoBacktransition(previousStatus, newStatus) and surfaces the verbatim error message 'Closed cases are terminal and cannot be modified.' via gs.addErrorMessage() + current.setAbortAction(true) when the validator returns {ok: false}. Per AAP Sections 0.5.5 (transition matrix row 8: 'Closed -> *: PROHIBITED -- terminal state') and 0.7.4 (verbatim error message - non-negotiable). Order=100 is the HIGHEST priority before-update rule in this scoped application; runs FIRST so all other guards/housekeeping rules (block_draft_backtransition at 200, validate_assigned_agent_membership at 300, clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress at 400, set_closed_date at 500) are short-circuited if the case is already Closed. The Script Include is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the verbatim error message; this BR contains zero inline error strings. action_insert=false because new cases always start at Draft (per dictionary default_value); only updates on already-Closed rows trigger this guard. Defensive previous-null guard handles async/system contexts where previous can be null. Script Include's validateNoBacktransition returns {ok: true} for the no-op Closed -> Closed case (status field unchanged), so non-status edits to a Closed row pass through; only actual transitions out of Closed are blocked. Type-agnostic: fires for both General Inquiry and Complaint case types. Defense in depth: the per-type Flow Designer flows do NOT enforce this rule, so this BR is the only enforcer for direct DB modifications via Table API, scripted REST, GlideRecord scripts, and bulk imports. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint and zero hardcoded sys_id literals.</description>
        <execute_function>false</execute_function>
        <filter_condition/>
        <is_rest>false</is_rest>
        <message/>
        <name>x_casemgmt_block_terminal_closed</name>
        <order>100</order>
        <priority>100</priority>
        <rest_method/>
        <rest_method_text/>
        <rest_service/>
        <rest_service_text/>
        <rest_variables/>
        <role_conditions/>
        <script><![CDATA[(function executeRule(current, previous /*null when async*/) {
    // Per AAP Section 0.5.5 (transition matrix row 8):
    //     "Closed -> *: PROHIBITED -- terminal state"
    //     Failure surface: "Closed cases are terminal and cannot be modified."
    //
    // Per AAP Section 0.7.4: the literal text "Closed cases are terminal and
    // cannot be modified." is the VERBATIM, NON-NEGOTIABLE error message
    // surfaced when this guard fires. Character-for-character: capital "C",
    // trailing period, exact wording. The message lives ONCE in the Script
    // Include's validateNoBacktransition() return; this BR reads it from
    // result.error and never inlines it.
    //
    // Order=100 makes this the FIRST/HIGHEST-PRIORITY before-update rule
    // in this scoped application. Subsequent rules
    // (block_draft_backtransition at 200, validate_assigned_agent_membership
    // at 300, clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress at 400, set_closed_date at
    // 500) are short-circuited when this rule aborts via setAbortAction(true).
    // Running first is intentional: it is the cheapest path to reject an
    // invalid update, and skipping the downstream rules saves database
    // queries and CPU.
    //
    // Delegates to x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.validateNoBacktransition()
    // which returns { ok: true } when the transition is allowed, or
    // { ok: false, error: 'Closed cases are terminal and cannot be modified.' }
    // when transitioning OUT of Closed (i.e., prev === 'Closed' &&
    // next !== 'Closed' && next !== ''). The Script Include is the single
    // source of truth for the verbatim message and the prohibited-transition
    // matrix; this BR consumes its return shape and surfaces the error.
    //
    // ArkCase source-side analog: ChangeCaseFileStateService at
    // acm-plugins/acm-default-plugins/acm-case-file-plugin/src/main/java/com/
    // armedia/acm/plugins/casefile/service/ChangeCaseFileStateService.java
    // handled state changes via changeCaseState() (line 95: setStatus,
    // line 99: setClosed for CLOSED transitions) but did NOT enforce a
    // "Closed is terminal" rule - the ArkCase service would happily transition
    // a Closed case to any other status. Terminal-state behavior was implicit
    // in Activiti BPMN process definitions and queue handlers. This BR makes
    // the rule EXPLICIT at the data layer, hardening the state-machine contract
    // per AAP Section 0.5.5.

    // Defensive previous-null guard: the `previous` GlideRecord is null in
    // certain async/system contexts (scheduled jobs invoking
    // GlideRecord.update(), some bulk import scenarios, server-side script
    // paths that call GlideRecord.updateMultiple()). If there is no previous
    // state, no terminal state can be detected, and the rule does nothing.
    if (!previous) {
        return;
    }

    // String() cast normalizes the GlideElement to a primitive string for
    // safe === comparison; without the cast, GlideElement-vs-string equality
    // can behave inconsistently across releases. The Title Case literal
    // 'Closed' (6 chars, single word) is the verbatim choice value from
    // ../choices/sys_choice_case_status.xml per AAP Section 0.5.7. Variants
    // like 'CLOSED' (uppercase, ArkCase Java legacy), 'closed' (lowercase),
    // or 'Close' (no trailing 'd') would silently never match.
    var prevStatus = String(previous.status);
    var newStatus = String(current.status);

    // Only fire when previous.status is Closed. The Script Include returns
    // { ok: false, error: 'Closed cases are terminal and cannot be modified.' }
    // when prev === 'Closed' && next !== 'Closed' && next !== ''. The Script
    // Include returns { ok: true } for the no-op Closed -> Closed case (status
    // unchanged - non-status edits to a Closed row are not blocked here; if
    // additional per-field immutability is required, dictionary read_only
    // flags or field-level ACLs are the right mechanism).
    if (prevStatus === 'Closed') {
        // Resolve the Script Include via the standard scoped reference syntax.
        // The `x_casemgmt` portion is the scope's vendor_prefix from
        // ../app/sys_app/x_casemgmt_case_management.xml; the `CaseTransitionValidator`
        // portion is the api_name of the Script Include. NO sys_id literal -
        // the resolution is by scoped class name only.
        var validator = new x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator();
        var result = validator.validateNoBacktransition(prevStatus, newStatus);
        if (!result.ok) {
            // gs.addErrorMessage(result.error) surfaces the verbatim message
            // at the top of the form when the user attempts the save through
            // the UI, and is captured in the API response when the update
            // arrives via Table API or scripted REST. The string is read from
            // result.error - never inlined here - so the verbatim wording
            // contract is enforced by the Script Include alone.
            gs.addErrorMessage(result.error);
            // current.setAbortAction(true) cancels the database write. Both
            // the gs.addErrorMessage() and setAbortAction(true) calls are
            // required: the former produces a visible error to the caller,
            // the latter prevents the write from being persisted. Without
            // setAbortAction(true), the message would appear but the row
            // would still update; without gs.addErrorMessage(), the write
            // would be silently rejected with no visible failure.
            current.setAbortAction(true);
        }
    }
})(current, previous);
]]></script>
        <sys_class_name>sys_script</sys_class_name>
        <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by>
        <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on>
        <sys_domain>global</sys_domain>
        <sys_domain_path>/</sys_domain_path>
        <sys_id>0ee339c18d12366737a3637b952fe75b</sys_id>
        <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count>
        <sys_name>x_casemgmt_block_terminal_closed</sys_name>
        <sys_overrides/>
        <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package>
        <sys_policy/>
        <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope>
        <sys_update_name>sys_script_x_casemgmt_block_terminal_closed</sys_update_name>
        <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by>
        <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on>
        <template/>
        <when>before</when>
    </sys_script>
</record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_set_opened_date
sys_update_xml.payload:
<record_update table="sys_script">
    <sys_script action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE">
        <abort_action>false</abort_action>
        <access>package_private</access>
        <action_delete>false</action_delete>
        <action_insert>true</action_insert>
        <action_query>false</action_query>
        <action_update>false</action_update>
        <active>true</active>
        <add_message>false</add_message>
        <advanced>true</advanced>
        <change_fields>false</change_fields>
        <client_callable>false</client_callable>
        <collection>x_casemgmt_case</collection>
        <condition/>
        <description>Before-insert on x_casemgmt_case: sets current.opened_date = gs.nowDateTime() when not already populated. Replaces ArkCase CaseFile.@PrePersist beforeInsert() audit hook from acm-plugins/acm-default-plugins/acm-case-file-plugin/src/main/java/com/armedia/acm/plugins/casefile/model/CaseFile.java. Per AAP Section 0.5.7 (opened_date Auto-set on creation). Defensive: respects pre-set values from seed data and other authoritative callers via current.opened_date.nil() guard. Fires in all contexts (form, Table API, scripted REST, seed scripts) because is_rest=false. Order=100 ensures the field is populated early in the before-insert phase. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint.</description>
        <execute_function>false</execute_function>
        <filter_condition/>
        <is_rest>false</is_rest>
        <message/>
        <name>x_casemgmt_set_opened_date</name>
        <order>100</order>
        <priority>100</priority>
        <rest_method/>
        <rest_method_text/>
        <rest_service/>
        <rest_service_text/>
        <rest_variables/>
        <role_conditions/>
        <script><![CDATA[(function executeRule(current, previous /*null when async*/) {
    // ServiceNow-native equivalent of ArkCase CaseFile.@PrePersist beforeInsert()
    // (acm-plugins/acm-default-plugins/acm-case-file-plugin/src/main/java/com/
    //  armedia/acm/plugins/casefile/model/CaseFile.java, lines 307-316).
    //
    // Per AAP Section 0.5.7: opened_date is "DateTime, Auto-set on creation".
    //
    // Defensive .nil() guard: respect pre-set values from authoritative callers.
    // This is critical for the seed-data demos in
    // ../seed-data/cases/x_casemgmt_case_demo_*.xml which explicitly set
    // synthetic timestamps spanning the past 30 days so the "Cases opened
    // (30 days)" single-score widget on the Manager View dashboard
    // (../reports/x_casemgmt_cases_opened_30d.xml) has non-zero data on a
    // freshly-imported PDI. Without this guard, the seeded timestamps would
    // be overwritten with gs.nowDateTime() at import time and every demo
    // case would appear to be opened on import day.
    //
    // For organic inserts (form submissions, Table API calls, portal scripted
    // REST submissions, ad-hoc admin scripts) where opened_date is not
    // pre-populated, the script populates the field with the current platform
    // time so downstream reports and the portal status lookup never see a
    // null opened_date.
    if (current.opened_date.nil()) {
        current.opened_date = gs.nowDateTime();
    }
})(current, previous);
]]></script>
        <sys_class_name>sys_script</sys_class_name>
        <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by>
        <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on>
        <sys_domain>global</sys_domain>
        <sys_domain_path>/</sys_domain_path>
        <sys_id>c56aeecd4086f111be2867faae9e55ad</sys_id>
        <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count>
        <sys_name>x_casemgmt_set_opened_date</sys_name>
        <sys_overrides/>
        <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package>
        <sys_policy/>
        <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope>
        <sys_update_name>sys_script_x_casemgmt_set_opened_date</sys_update_name>
        <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by>
        <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on>
        <template/>
        <when>before</when>
    </sys_script>
</record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_set_closed_date
sys_update_xml.payload:
<record_update table="sys_script">
    <sys_script action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE">
        <abort_action>false</abort_action>
        <access>package_private</access>
        <action_delete>false</action_delete>
        <action_insert>false</action_insert>
        <action_query>false</action_query>
        <action_update>true</action_update>
        <active>true</active>
        <add_message>false</add_message>
        <advanced>true</advanced>
        <change_fields>false</change_fields>
        <client_callable>false</client_callable>
        <collection>x_casemgmt_case</collection>
        <condition/>
        <description>Before-update on x_casemgmt_case: when previous.status == 'Resolved' AND current.status == 'Closed', sets current.closed_date = gs.nowDateTime() if not already populated. Replaces ArkCase ChangeCaseFileStateService.changeCaseState() retval.setClosed(now) (line 99 in acm-plugins/acm-default-plugins/acm-case-file-plugin/src/main/java/com/armedia/acm/plugins/casefile/service/ChangeCaseFileStateService.java). Per AAP Section 0.5.5 (Resolved->Closed auto-sets closed_date) and AAP Section 0.5.7 (closed_date DateTime, Auto-set on Close transition). Order=500 ensures this rule runs LAST among before-update rules so it only fires after all transition guards (block_terminal_closed, block_draft_backtransition, validate_assigned_agent_membership, clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress) have passed. Defensive .nil() guard preserves authoritative pre-set values from seed data and the validate_closed_transition Flow Designer subflow. Defense in depth with the subflow is intentional - this BR catches direct DB updates (Table API, scripted REST, GlideRecord scripts), the subflow catches interactive transitions through the form/UI. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint.</description>
        <execute_function>false</execute_function>
        <filter_condition/>
        <is_rest>false</is_rest>
        <message/>
        <name>x_casemgmt_set_closed_date</name>
        <order>500</order>
        <priority>100</priority>
        <rest_method/>
        <rest_method_text/>
        <rest_service/>
        <rest_service_text/>
        <rest_variables/>
        <role_conditions/>
        <script><![CDATA[(function executeRule(current, previous /*null when async*/) {
    // ServiceNow-native equivalent of ArkCase
    // ChangeCaseFileStateService.changeCaseState() line 99:
    //     if ("CLOSED".equals(newState)) { retval.setClosed(now); }
    // (acm-plugins/acm-default-plugins/acm-case-file-plugin/src/main/java/com/
    //  armedia/acm/plugins/casefile/service/ChangeCaseFileStateService.java)
    //
    // Per AAP Section 0.5.5: Resolved -> Closed transition auto-sets closed_date.
    // Per AAP Section 0.5.7: closed_date is "DateTime, Auto-set on Close transition".
    //
    // This BR runs alongside the Flow Designer subflow
    // ../flows/sub_flows/validate_closed_transition.xml; the redundancy is
    // intentional - this BR catches direct DB modifications (Table API,
    // scripted REST, GlideRecord scripts, bulk imports), the flow catches
    // interactive transitions through the form/UI.
    //
    // Order=500 ensures this rule runs LAST among before-update rules; if
    // any earlier guard aborted the action (block_terminal_closed at
    // order=100, block_draft_backtransition at order=200,
    // validate_assigned_agent_membership at order=300,
    // clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress at order=400), this BR is skipped.
    // We never stamp closed_date on a transition that's about to be
    // rejected.
    //
    // Defensive previous-null guard: the `previous` GlideRecord is null in
    // certain async/system contexts. If there is no previous state, no
    // transition can be detected, and the rule does nothing. The String()
    // cast normalizes the GlideElement to a primitive string for safe
    // === comparison; without the cast, GlideElement-vs-string equality
    // can behave inconsistently across releases.
    var prevStatus = previous ? String(previous.status) : '';
    var newStatus = String(current.status);

    // Verbatim Title Case status values per AAP Section 0.5.7 and the
    // sibling choice list ../choices/sys_choice_case_status.xml. The
    // labels ARE the stored values - ServiceNow Choice fields persist
    // the LABEL string by default, not an integer ID.
    if (prevStatus === 'Resolved' && newStatus === 'Closed') {
        // Defensive .nil() guard: respect pre-set values from the
        // Flow Designer subflow (which fires in the same transaction
        // for interactive transitions and may have already stamped
        // the column) and from any future seed-data path that updates
        // an already-loaded case from Resolved to Closed. Without this
        // guard, a value already written by the subflow would be
        // overwritten by gs.nowDateTime() at this rule's execution
        // point - harmless in practice (both writers produce the same
        // millisecond-precision timestamp within a single transaction)
        // but unnecessary and slightly less clear in audit logs.
        if (current.closed_date.nil()) {
            current.closed_date = gs.nowDateTime();
        }
    }
})(current, previous);
]]></script>
        <sys_class_name>sys_script</sys_class_name>
        <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by>
        <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on>
        <sys_domain>global</sys_domain>
        <sys_domain_path>/</sys_domain_path>
        <sys_id>f6d63b3856121cac9b69215b2c46f64c</sys_id>
        <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count>
        <sys_name>x_casemgmt_set_closed_date</sys_name>
        <sys_overrides/>
        <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package>
        <sys_policy/>
        <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope>
        <sys_update_name>sys_script_x_casemgmt_set_closed_date</sys_update_name>
        <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by>
        <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on>
        <template/>
        <when>before</when>
    </sys_script>
</record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_validate_assigned_agent_membership
sys_update_xml.payload:
<record_update table="sys_script">
    <sys_script action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE">
        <abort_action>false</abort_action>
        <access>package_private</access>
        <action_delete>false</action_delete>
        <action_insert>true</action_insert>
        <action_query>false</action_query>
        <action_update>true</action_update>
        <active>true</active>
        <add_message>false</add_message>
        <advanced>true</advanced>
        <change_fields>false</change_fields>
        <client_callable>false</client_callable>
        <collection>x_casemgmt_case</collection>
        <condition/>
        <description>Before-insert/update on x_casemgmt_case: when assigned_agent and assigned_group are both populated, validates that the agent is a member of the group via x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.isAgentInGroup(). On failure, surfaces the form-level error 'Assigned agent must be a member of the assigned group.' via gs.addErrorMessage() + current.setAbortAction(true). Per AAP Sections 0.5.5 (transition matrix row 2: Open -> In Progress: 'assigned_agent populated AND member of assigned_group') and 0.5.7 (assigned_agent constraint: 'Optional; must be member of assigned_group'). Order=300 places this rule THIRD in the before-rule chain on x_casemgmt_case, after block_terminal_closed (100) and block_draft_backtransition (200) and before clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress (400) and set_closed_date (500); running after the prohibition guards is a deliberate performance optimization so we don't run the sys_user_grmember GlideRecord lookup on transitions that will be rejected anyway. Dual-triggered (action_insert=true AND action_update=true) because a manager (or seed script) may assign agent and group at case-creation time via a single Table API call, not just through subsequent updates. Skip conditions: (a) either agent or group is empty (b) neither field changed since previous - avoid redundant queries on non-assignment-touching updates. Defensive previous-null guard: on insert previous is null and both change-detection flags evaluate true so the membership is always validated for new rows. Type-agnostic: fires for both General Inquiry and Complaint case types because the membership constraint applies regardless of case type. Defense in depth: validate_inprogress_transition subflow makes the same check at the workflow layer; this BR catches direct DB writes bypassing the flow. The error message wording is NOT prescribed verbatim by AAP Section 0.7.4 (which lists only four verbatim messages); the chosen wording is consistent with the prompt's tone and the AAP's own narrative wording. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint and zero hardcoded sys_id literals (runtime sys_id comparisons via .toString() are platform-canonical and permitted).</description>
        <execute_function>false</execute_function>
        <filter_condition/>
        <is_rest>false</is_rest>
        <message/>
        <name>x_casemgmt_validate_assigned_agent_membership</name>
        <order>300</order>
        <priority>100</priority>
        <rest_method/>
        <rest_method_text/>
        <rest_service/>
        <rest_service_text/>
        <rest_variables/>
        <role_conditions/>
        <script><![CDATA[(function executeRule(current, previous /*null when async*/) {
    // Per AAP Section 0.5.7 (x_casemgmt_case schema row 10):
    //     "assigned_agent | Reference -> sys_user | Optional; must be
    //      member of assigned_group"
    //
    // Per AAP Section 0.5.5 (transition matrix row 2):
    //     | From | To          | Required condition                       | Failure handling          |
    //     | Open | In Progress | assigned_agent populated AND member of   | Surface form-level error  |
    //     |      |             | assigned_group                           |                           |
    //
    // The membership check is delegated to
    //     new x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator().isAgentInGroup(agentSysId, groupSysId)
    // which performs a sys_user_grmember GlideRecord lookup and returns a
    // plain boolean. The Script Include is the single source of truth for
    // the membership query so the same logic is reusable across this BR,
    // the validate_inprogress_transition Flow Designer subflow, ACL
    // conditions for child case_task and case_party rows, and the
    // seed-script preflight checks. Inlining a GlideRecord('sys_user_grmember')
    // query here would create a second source of truth and risk the two
    // implementations diverging on edge cases.
    //
    // Order=300 places this rule THIRD in the before-rule chain on
    // x_casemgmt_case, after block_terminal_closed (order=100, blocks any
    // edit to a Closed case) and block_draft_backtransition (order=200,
    // blocks any rewind to Draft). The chain order is a deliberate
    // performance optimization: invalid transitions short-circuit at the
    // earlier orders via setAbortAction(true) and we never reach this rule's
    // GlideRecord query for them. Cases that survive to order=300 have a
    // legitimate forward-transition or non-status edit, so the membership
    // check is appropriate. Subsequent rules
    // (clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress at order=400, set_closed_date at
    // order=500) are short-circuited if THIS rule aborts.
    //
    // ArkCase source-side analog: there was no direct equivalent at the JPA
    // entity layer in
    //   acm-plugins/acm-default-plugins/acm-case-file-plugin/src/main/java/
    //     com/armedia/acm/plugins/casefile/model/CaseFile.java
    // CaseFile modeled assignment via an AcmParticipant collection (lines
    // 192, 336-340, 880-908) where ASSIGNEE and OWNING_GROUP participant
    // types coexisted on the case but the relational integrity between an
    // assignee and the owning group was enforced indirectly through the
    // LDAP/AD organizational structure - if a user existed in LDAP they
    // could be assigned regardless of their group membership. The
    // ServiceNow target makes this rule EXPLICIT at the data layer via
    // this BR plus the Script Include's isAgentInGroup() helper - a
    // deliberate hardening of the case assignment contract. This is a
    // TARGET-SIDE AUTHORED rule with no byte-for-byte source analog.

    // Read the assigned_agent and assigned_group sys_id values from the
    // platform-injected `current` GlideRecord. The .toString() coercion
    // normalizes the GlideElement reference field to its underlying sys_id
    // string. Without explicit coercion, the GlideElement instance auto-
    // converts in some contexts but not others (string concatenation =
    // string conversion; equality comparison via === = identity comparison;
    // method-argument passing = pass-by-value of the underlying string
    // for primitive comparisons but pass-by-reference of the GlideElement
    // wrapper for complex operations). The downstream isAgentInGroup()
    // method explicitly handles String() coercion of its arguments and
    // GlideRecord.addQuery() accepts both forms, but explicit .toString()
    // here is the platform-canonical defensive pattern and avoids any
    // wrapper-vs-primitive ambiguity downstream.
    var agent = current.assigned_agent.toString();
    var group = current.assigned_group.toString();

    // Skip if either field is empty. The AAP defines assigned_agent as
    // optional (Section 0.5.7: "Optional"); a case without an assigned
    // agent is a valid state (e.g., Draft cases, Open cases waiting for
    // agent assignment). assigned_group is mandatory only on the
    // Open transition (per dictionary mandatory_on_open=true at
    // ../dictionary/x_casemgmt_case_assigned_group.xml) - that mandatory
    // requirement is enforced separately by the form-level mandatory flag
    // and the validate_open_transition subflow, NOT by this BR.
    // This BR's responsibility is narrow: validate the membership
    // RELATIONSHIP when both fields are present. If either field is
    // absent, the relationship cannot be validated and the rule does
    // nothing.
    if (!agent || !group) {
        return;
    }

    // Skip if neither agent nor group changed (avoid redundant queries on
    // every save, including subsequent updates that touch only other
    // fields like description, priority, or status). For a case that
    // travels through the full state-machine
    //   Draft -> Open -> In Progress -> Pending -> In Progress -> Resolved -> Closed
    // the assignment fields typically change at most twice (once when the
    // group is set on Draft -> Open, once when the agent is set on
    // Open -> In Progress). This guard ensures the BR runs the
    // sys_user_grmember query at most twice across the full lifecycle
    // rather than five times.
    //
    // On INSERT, `previous` is null (no prior state), so the ternary's
    // !previous branch evaluates true for both flags - the BR always
    // validates the membership on a brand-new row. This is the correct
    // semantic: a manager creating a case AND assigning both group and
    // agent in a single operation must have the assignment validated
    // before the row is persisted, otherwise an invalid agent-group
    // pairing could be created and only caught on the first subsequent
    // update.
    var agentChanged = !previous || String(previous.assigned_agent) !== agent;
    var groupChanged = !previous || String(previous.assigned_group) !== group;
    if (!agentChanged && !groupChanged) {
        return;
    }

    // Resolve the Script Include via the standard scoped reference syntax.
    // The `x_casemgmt` portion is the scope's vendor_prefix from
    // ../app/sys_app/x_casemgmt_case_management.xml; the
    // `CaseTransitionValidator` portion is the api_name of the Script
    // Include declared in
    // ../script_includes/x_casemgmt_CaseTransitionValidator.xml at
    // <api_name>x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator</api_name>. NO sys_id
    // literal - the resolution is by scoped class name only.
    var validator = new x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator();

    // Call isAgentInGroup(userSysId, groupSysId). The Script Include's
    // method (line 656 in CaseTransitionValidator.xml) returns a plain
    // boolean: true when at least one sys_user_grmember row links the
    // given user to the given group, false otherwise (including when
    // either argument is null/empty/whitespace). The boolean shape
    // (rather than the {ok, error} pair used by validateNoBacktransition
    // and the canTransitionTo* family) is intentional: isAgentInGroup is
    // a simple yes/no membership predicate reusable from contexts that
    // have no error-message surface (e.g., ACL condition expressions).
    // The form-level error string is owned by THIS BR and surfaced via
    // gs.addErrorMessage(...) below.
    if (!validator.isAgentInGroup(agent, group)) {
        // gs.addErrorMessage('...') surfaces the form-level error at the
        // top of the case form when the user attempts the save through
        // the UI, and is captured in the API response when the update
        // arrives via Table API or scripted REST. Per AAP Section 0.7.1:
        // "Surface all blocking errors on the form. Invalid transition
        // attempts MUST produce a blocking error message visible on the
        // form - silent failures or post-save error logs are
        // unacceptable."
        //
        // Per AAP Section 0.7.4, only four error messages are verbatim
        // (non-negotiable): "All tasks must be closed before resolving
        // this case.", "Cases cannot be returned to Draft.", "Closed
        // cases are terminal and cannot be modified.", and "No case
        // found with that number." This rule's message is NOT in that
        // list, so the wording here is target-side authored. The chosen
        // string is consistent with the prompt's tone and the AAP's own
        // narrative wording in Section 0.5.7 ("Optional; must be member
        // of assigned_group").
        gs.addErrorMessage('Assigned agent must be a member of the assigned group.');

        // current.setAbortAction(true) cancels the database write. Both
        // the gs.addErrorMessage() and setAbortAction(true) calls are
        // required: the former produces a visible error to the caller,
        // the latter prevents the write from being persisted. Without
        // setAbortAction(true), the message would appear but the row
        // would still update with the invalid agent-group pairing;
        // without gs.addErrorMessage(), the write would be silently
        // rejected with no visible failure and no diagnostic message
        // for the caller.
        current.setAbortAction(true);
    }
})(current, previous);
]]></script>
        <sys_class_name>sys_script</sys_class_name>
        <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by>
        <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on>
        <sys_domain>global</sys_domain>
        <sys_domain_path>/</sys_domain_path>
        <sys_id>8136e6a730a2da3c4c36038f572c4269</sys_id>
        <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count>
        <sys_name>x_casemgmt_validate_assigned_agent_membership</sys_name>
        <sys_overrides/>
        <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package>
        <sys_policy/>
        <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope>
        <sys_update_name>sys_script_x_casemgmt_validate_assigned_agent_membership</sys_update_name>
        <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by>
        <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on>
        <template/>
        <when>before</when>
    </sys_script>
</record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress
sys_update_xml.payload:
<record_update table="sys_script">
    <sys_script action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE">
        <abort_action>false</abort_action>
        <access>package_private</access>
        <action_delete>false</action_delete>
        <action_insert>false</action_insert>
        <action_query>false</action_query>
        <action_update>true</action_update>
        <active>true</active>
        <add_message>false</add_message>
        <advanced>true</advanced>
        <change_fields>false</change_fields>
        <client_callable>false</client_callable>
        <collection>x_casemgmt_case</collection>
        <condition/>
        <description>Before-update on x_casemgmt_case: when previous.status == 'Pending' AND current.status == 'In Progress', clears current.pending_reason (sets it to empty string). Per AAP Section 0.5.5 (Pending->In Progress: clears pending_reason). Order=400 ensures this rule runs after the transition guards (block_terminal_closed at order=100, block_draft_backtransition at order=200, validate_assigned_agent_membership at order=300) but before set_closed_date (order=500), so it only fires after all guards have approved the transition. Companion to ../flows/sub_flows/validate_pending_transition.xml which SETS pending_reason on the In Progress->Pending transition; this rule CLEARS it on the reverse transition. Type-agnostic: fires for both General Inquiry and Complaint case types since the Pending state is shared. Defensive previous-null guard handles async/system contexts where previous can be null. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint and zero GlideRecord lookups.</description>
        <execute_function>false</execute_function>
        <filter_condition/>
        <is_rest>false</is_rest>
        <message/>
        <name>x_casemgmt_clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress</name>
        <order>400</order>
        <priority>100</priority>
        <rest_method/>
        <rest_method_text/>
        <rest_service/>
        <rest_service_text/>
        <rest_variables/>
        <role_conditions/>
        <script><![CDATA[(function executeRule(current, previous /*null when async*/) {
    // Per AAP Section 0.5.5 verbatim:
    //     "Pending -> In Progress: None; clears pending_reason"
    //
    // The pending_reason field is SET by the Flow Designer subflow
    // ../flows/sub_flows/validate_pending_transition.xml when the case
    // transitions INTO Pending. When the case transitions OUT of Pending
    // back to In Progress, the previously stored reason is no longer
    // relevant and would be misleading on the form. This rule guarantees
    // the field is empty for any non-Pending case reached via the
    // Pending -> In Progress transition path.
    //
    // This BR has no ArkCase source-side analog. ArkCase did not have
    // a discrete Pending sub-state with a typed reason code; this is
    // target-side-only behavior introduced by the AAP design.
    //
    // Order=400 ensures this rule runs AFTER all transition guards
    // (block_terminal_closed at order=100, block_draft_backtransition at
    // order=200, validate_assigned_agent_membership at order=300) have
    // approved the transition, and BEFORE set_closed_date (order=500)
    // which handles the Resolved -> Closed side-effect. If any earlier
    // guard aborts the action, this rule is skipped (the platform
    // short-circuits the BR pipeline on abort) - which is correct,
    // because we never want to clear pending_reason on a transition
    // that's about to be rejected.
    //
    // Defensive previous-null guard: the `previous` GlideRecord is null
    // in certain async/system contexts. If there is no previous state,
    // no transition can be detected, and the rule does nothing. The
    // String() cast normalizes the GlideElement to a primitive string
    // for safe === comparison; without the cast, GlideElement-vs-string
    // equality can behave inconsistently across releases.
    var prevStatus = previous ? String(previous.status) : '';
    var newStatus = String(current.status);

    // Verbatim Title Case status values per AAP Section 0.5.7 and the
    // sibling choice list ../choices/sys_choice_case_status.xml. The
    // labels ARE the stored values - ServiceNow Choice fields persist
    // the LABEL string by default, not an integer ID. Note 'In Progress'
    // contains a literal SPACE in the middle (NOT 'In_Progress', NOT
    // 'InProgress', NOT 'in progress'). Misspelled variants would
    // silently never match and the rule would never fire - a critical
    // correctness concern that is encoded by these exact string literals.
    if (prevStatus === 'Pending' && newStatus === 'In Progress') {
        // The empty-string assignment is the canonical ServiceNow pattern
        // for clearing a Choice field's value. Per
        // ../dictionary/x_casemgmt_case_pending_reason.xml the field is
        // non-mandatory (mandatory=false), so clearing it is a legitimate
        // end-state - the row remains valid with an empty pending_reason
        // for any non-Pending status. The next time the case transitions
        // to Pending, the validate_pending_transition subflow will prompt
        // for a fresh reason and populate the field again.
        current.pending_reason = '';
    }
})(current, previous);
]]></script>
        <sys_class_name>sys_script</sys_class_name>
        <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by>
        <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on>
        <sys_domain>global</sys_domain>
        <sys_domain_path>/</sys_domain_path>
        <sys_id>0943b10691d8ae86fd3c2fd6994eb693</sys_id>
        <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count>
        <sys_name>x_casemgmt_clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress</sys_name>
        <sys_overrides/>
        <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package>
        <sys_policy/>
        <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope>
        <sys_update_name>sys_script_x_casemgmt_clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress</sys_update_name>

    </sys_script>
