sys_update_xml.name: Case Party Conditional Fields
sys_update_xml.payload:
true party_typeISNOTEMPTY^ORparty_typeISEMPTY^EQ Polymorphic field-rendering UI Policy on x_casemgmt_case_party. Implements the ServiceNow-native equivalent of ArkCase's @DiscriminatorColumn(cm_class_name) single-table inheritance pattern from PersonAssociation.java and the dual-table @ManyToOne pattern from PersonOrganizationAssociation.java per AAP Section 0.4.3 ("Polymorphic association via single-table choice + UI Policy"). Uses run_scripts=true with an imperative onCondition script to deterministically set field visibility and mandatory state for all three party_type values: "Person" (show person + mandatory; hide organization), "Organization" (show organization + mandatory; hide person), and empty (hide both fields, both not mandatory). The script_true block is the sole logic path; no declarative actions are used because the declarative two-policy approach with reverse_if_false=true has an unavoidable order-conflict in the empty-state case. Runs on form load (on_load=true) so existing case_party records render correctly when opened. The party_type field itself is mandatory at the dictionary level (../dictionary/x_casemgmt_case_party_party_type.xml), so users cannot save a record without picking one; the mandatory state of person/organization is therefore conditional on party_type and enforced ONLY through this UI Policy. true false Case Party Conditional Fields true 100 false true Polymorphic field rendering: show person+mandatory when party_type=Person, show organization+mandatory when party_type=Organization, hide both when party_type is empty. sys_ui_policy admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 global 854295ebb4c8309eea6a52aa788ec8a9 0 x_casemgmt x_casemgmt admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case_party
0

sys_update_xml.name: Open
sys_update_xml.payload:
x_casemgmt_case_open true false UI Action that transitions a Draft case to Open status. Visible only to x_casemgmt_case_manager users on cases with status=Draft. Delegates the assigned_group-populated check to x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToOpen() (single source of truth shared with ../flows/sub_flows/validate_open_transition.xml). Per AAP Section 0.5.5 row 1 verbatim: "Draft -> Open: assigned_group populated - Surface form-level error". Server-side-only (no client prompt) - the transition has no required parameters beyond the assigned_group field that the operator should have populated on the form before clicking. Performs the transition on the server with defense-in-depth re-validation of role and status. Surfaces a form-level blocking error via gs.addErrorMessage if assigned_group is empty (verbatim message "Required field assigned_group is empty." returned by the Script Include). Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint, zero hardcoded sys_id literals, and zero PII. true true false false false false primary false Open this case (assigned_group must be populated; case_manager role required). true false false false false false false false Open 100 x_casemgmt_case_manager false false false true sys_ui_action admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 global / 2d897ab4b680731b3da534c9a667c658 0 x_casemgmt_case_open x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_ui_action_x_casemgmt_case_open admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
true true

sys_update_xml.name: Start Progress
sys_update_xml.payload:
x_casemgmt_case_start_progress true false UI Action that transitions an Open case to In Progress status. Visible to x_casemgmt_case_manager (always) and x_casemgmt_case_agent (only when they are assigned to the case via assigned_agent or member of assigned_group, per AAP Section 0.5.6 Assigned-only rule). Server-side-only (no client prompt) - the transition has no required parameters beyond the assigned_agent and assigned_group fields that the operator should have populated on the form before clicking. Delegates the assigned_agent-populated AND member-of-assigned_group check to x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToInProgress() (single source of truth shared with ../flows/sub_flows/validate_inprogress_transition.xml and ../business_rules/x_casemgmt_validate_assigned_agent_membership.xml). Per AAP Section 0.5.5 row 2 verbatim: "Open -> In Progress: assigned_agent populated AND member of assigned_group - Surface form-level error". Performs the transition on the server with defense-in-depth re-validation of role, status, and "Assigned only" assignment. Surfaces a form-level blocking error via gs.addErrorMessage if any guard fails (verbatim message returned by the Script Include). Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint, zero hardcoded sys_id literals, and zero PII. true true false false false false primary false Start working on this case (assigned_agent must be populated and a member of assigned_group). true false false false false false false false Start Progress 200 x_casemgmt_case_manager,x_casemgmt_case_agent false false false true sys_ui_action admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 global / e77dc24317a301765a60a3673ec7d164 0 x_casemgmt_case_start_progress x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_ui_action_x_casemgmt_case_start_progress admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
true true

sys_update_xml.name: Set Pending
sys_update_xml.payload:
x_casemgmt_case_set_pending true true UI Action that transitions an In Progress case to Pending status. Visible to x_casemgmt_case_manager (always) and x_casemgmt_case_agent (only when assigned to the case via assigned_agent or member of assigned_group, per AAP Section 0.5.6 Assigned-only rule). Prompts the user for pending_reason (Awaiting Info / Awaiting Third Party / Other) on the client via a hybrid client+server pattern, then performs the transition on the server with defense-in-depth re-validation of role, status, assignment, and pending_reason. Per AAP Section 0.5.5 row 3 verbatim: "In Progress -> Pending: None; sets pending_reason (Awaiting Info / Awaiting Third Party / Other)". Companion to ../business_rules/x_casemgmt_clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress.xml which clears pending_reason on the reverse transition, and ../flows/sub_flows/validate_pending_transition.xml which handles the same transition at the Flow Designer workflow layer. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint, zero hardcoded sys_id literals, and zero PII. true true false false false false secondary false Pause this case (provide a pending reason). true false false false false false false false Set Pending onClickSetPending() 300 x_casemgmt_case_manager,x_casemgmt_case_agent false false false true sys_ui_action admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 global / af34b659d531b7a994da7d1f6a5e09f1 0 x_casemgmt_case_set_pending x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_ui_action_x_casemgmt_case_set_pending admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
true true

sys_update_xml.name: Resume
sys_update_xml.payload:
x_casemgmt_case_resume true false UI Action that resumes a Pending case back to In Progress status, clearing pending_reason. Visible to x_casemgmt_case_manager (always) and x_casemgmt_case_agent (only when assigned to the case via assigned_agent or member of assigned_group, per AAP Section 0.5.6 Assigned-only rule). Server-side-only (no client prompt) - the transition has no required parameters per AAP Section 0.5.5 row 4 verbatim: "Pending -> In Progress: None; clears pending_reason". Performs the transition on the server with defense-in-depth re-validation of role, status, and assignment. Explicitly clears pending_reason in addition to the companion business rule ../business_rules/x_casemgmt_clear_pending_reason_on_inprogress.xml (order=400) which also clears it during the same database update transaction; together they form a belt-and-suspenders defense-in-depth pair. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint, zero hardcoded sys_id literals, and zero PII. true true false false false false primary false Resume work on this case (clears pending_reason). true false false false false false false false Resume 400 x_casemgmt_case_manager,x_casemgmt_case_agent false false false true sys_ui_action admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 global / d48fd05fbca31c2194cac4b8645a2dbc 0 x_casemgmt_case_resume x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_ui_action_x_casemgmt_case_resume admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
true true

sys_update_xml.name: Resolve
sys_update_xml.payload:
x_casemgmt_case_resolve true false UI Action that transitions an In Progress case to Resolved status. Visible to x_casemgmt_case_manager (always) and x_casemgmt_case_agent (only when they are assigned to the case via assigned_agent or member of assigned_group, per AAP Section 0.5.6 Assigned-only rule). Delegates the all-tasks-closed check to x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToResolved() (single source of truth shared with ../flows/sub_flows/validate_resolved_transition.xml). Per AAP Section 0.5.5 row 5 verbatim: "In Progress -> Resolved: All linked x_casemgmt_case_task records have status = Closed - Surface 'All tasks must be closed before resolving this case.'". Surfaces verbatim error message "All tasks must be closed before resolving this case." (per AAP Sections 0.5.5 and 0.7.4) when any child task has status != Closed. Server-side-only (no client prompt) - the transition has no required parameters beyond the existing child task rows on the case. Performs the transition on the server with defense-in-depth re-validation of role, status, and "Assigned only" assignment. Surfaces a form-level blocking error via gs.addErrorMessage if any guard fails (verbatim message returned by the Script Include). Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint, zero hardcoded sys_id literals, and zero PII. true true false false false false primary false Resolve this case (all child tasks must be Closed). true false false false false false false false Resolve 500 x_casemgmt_case_manager,x_casemgmt_case_agent false false false true sys_ui_action admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 global / fcef26b5dd69234a0b4a8ea82d997b17 0 x_casemgmt_case_resolve x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_ui_action_x_casemgmt_case_resolve admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
true true

sys_update_xml.name: Close
sys_update_xml.payload:
x_casemgmt_case_close true false UI Action that transitions a Resolved case to Closed status. Visible only to x_casemgmt_case_manager users on cases with status=Resolved (per AAP Section 0.5.5 row 6 verbatim: "Resolved -> Closed: Caller has x_casemgmt_case_manager role; auto-set closed_date - Surface form-level error"). Delegates the role check to x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToClosed() (single source of truth shared with ../flows/sub_flows/validate_closed_transition.xml). Server-side-only (no client prompt) - the transition has no required parameters beyond the existing Resolved status. Performs the transition on the server with defense-in-depth re-validation of role and status. Surfaces a form-level blocking error via gs.addErrorMessage if any guard fails (verbatim message "Only case managers can close cases." returned by the Script Include). The closed_date field is auto-populated by the cooperating ../business_rules/x_casemgmt_set_closed_date.xml before-update Business Rule (order=500) on the same current.update() transaction triggered by this UI Action - this UI Action sets only current.status='Closed', the BR sets current.closed_date=gs.nowDateTime(). After the transition completes, the ../business_rules/x_casemgmt_block_terminal_closed.xml before-update Business Rule (order=100) prevents any subsequent modifications to the case (per AAP Section 0.5.5 row 8 terminal-state rule). Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint, zero hardcoded sys_id literals, and zero PII. true true false false false false destructive false Close this case (case_manager role required; case must be in Resolved status). closed_date will be auto-populated. This action is TERMINAL - the case cannot be modified after closure. true false false false false false false false Close 600 x_casemgmt_case_manager false false false true sys_ui_action admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 global / c2988c82e1da1b3e1029196a6e0d0f9f 0 x_casemgmt_case_close x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_ui_action_x_casemgmt_case_close admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
true true

sys_update_xml.name: Case Management Portal
sys_update_xml.payload: <record_update table="sp_portal"> <sp_portal action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <active>true</active> <background_color/> <background_image/> <background_image_dv/> <catalog/> <catalog_dv/> <description>Unauthenticated Experience Portal for the Case Management scoped application. Provides two pages: case_submit (anonymous case submission) and case_status (anonymous case status lookup). Replaces ArkCase's acm-service-portal-gateway anonymous-submission pattern (PortalRequestService.submitRequest and PortalRequestService.getRequestStatus). Per AAP Section 0.4.4 (UI Design - default theme), Section 0.7.2 (deployment step 3 - portal URL), and the seven validation gates (Gate 4 portal-submission, Gate 5 portal-lookup). Public access is enabled via the public flag plus a login_page redirect target that points to the submission page itself, so anonymous callers never see a login prompt. The portal uses the platform default Service Portal theme (no custom CSS, no custom branding) per AAP Section 0.4.4. Knowledge-base and service-catalog integrations are out of scope per AAP Section 0.3.2 and are intentionally empty.</description> <enable_legacy_recents>false</enable_legacy_recents> <hide_portal_name>false</hide_portal_name> <homepage display_value="Case Submission">x_casemgmt_case_submit</homepage> <homepage_dv display_value="Case Submission">x_casemgmt_case_submit</homepage_dv> <icon/> <icon_dv/> <kb_categories_visible>false</kb_categories_visible> <knowledge_base/> <knowledge_base_dv/> <login_page display_value="Case Submission">x_casemgmt_case_submit</login_page> <login_page_dv display_value="Case Submission">x_casemgmt_case_submit</login_page_dv> <logo/> <logo_dv/> <public>true</public> <quick_start_guide_url/> <recents_count>0</recents_count> <show_more_threshold>0</show_more_threshold> <sys_class_name>sp_portal</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>82079e7877f4f6d09975ab96fc0fa2fa</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>x_casemgmt_case_portal</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>sp_portal_x_casemgmt_case_portal</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <theme/> <theme_dv/> <title>Case Management Portal</title> <typeahead_min_length>3</typeahead_min_length> <url_suffix>x_casemgmt_case_portal</url_suffix> </sp_portal> </record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: Submit a Case
sys_update_xml.payload: <record_update table="sp_page"> <sp_page action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <category/> <description>Unauthenticated Service Portal page for case submission. Embeds the case submission widget (sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_submission_widget) which renders a 5-field form (subject, type, description, requester_name, requester_email) and POSTs to /api/x_casemgmt/case_submit. On success, the embedded widget transitively displays the confirmation widget (sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget) with the returned case number and the verbatim message 'Your case has been submitted'. Member of the x_casemgmt_case_portal Experience Portal AND serves as the portal's homepage (the portal record's homepage_dv references this page's id). Replaces ArkCase's acm-service-portal-gateway anonymous-submission pattern. Per AAP Sections 0.4.4 and 0.7.4 and Validation Gate 4 (Portal - submission).</description> <draft>false</draft> <dynamic_title_structure/> <hide_for_kb>true</hide_for_kb> <id>x_casemgmt_case_submit</id> <internal>false</internal> <omit_watcher>true</omit_watcher> <page_internal><![CDATA[ { "containers": [ { "id": "container_main", "containerClass": "container", "rows": [ { "id": "row_submit", "columns": [ { "id": "col_submit", "size": 12, "instances": [ { "id": "instance_submission_widget", "widget_id": "x_casemgmt_case_submission_widget", "widgetReference": "x_casemgmt_case_submission_widget", "title": "Submit a Case", "options": {} } ] } ] } ] } ] } ]]></page_internal> <page_specific_css/> <public>true</public> <roles/> <sys_class_name>sp_page</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>6a1f3c2ffd653613304512a734aab83b</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Submit a Case</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>sp_page_x_casemgmt_case_submit</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <title>Submit a Case</title> </sp_page> </record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: Case Status Lookup
sys_update_xml.payload: <record_update table="sp_page"> <sp_page action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <category/> <description>Unauthenticated Service Portal page for case status lookup. Embeds the case lookup widget (sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget) which renders a single case-number input plus result panel showing ONLY status, subject, opened_date on match, or the verbatim text 'No case found with that number.' on no-match. Reachable via direct URL [instance URL]/x_casemgmt_case_portal?id=x_casemgmt_case_status. Member of the x_casemgmt_case_portal Experience Portal but NOT its homepage (the submission page is the homepage). Replaces ArkCase's GetCaseByNumberService and PortalRequestService.getRequestStatus anonymous-status-lookup pattern. Per AAP Sections 0.4.4 and 0.7.4 (verbatim no-case-found message) and Validation Gate 5 (Portal - lookup).</description> <draft>false</draft> <dynamic_title_structure/> <hide_for_kb>true</hide_for_kb> <id>x_casemgmt_case_status</id> <internal>false</internal> <omit_watcher>true</omit_watcher> <page_internal><![CDATA[ { "containers": [ { "id": "container_main", "containerClass": "container", "rows": [ { "id": "row_lookup", "columns": [ { "id": "col_lookup", "size": 12, "instances": [ { "id": "instance_lookup_widget", "widget_id": "x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget", "widgetReference": "x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget", "title": "Case Status Lookup", "options": {} } ] } ] } ] } ] } ]]></page_internal> <page_specific_css/> <public>true</public> <roles/> <sys_class_name>sp_page</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>8881e418f5d24b17b641505a4f1dc814</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Case Status Lookup</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>sp_page_x_casemgmt_case_status</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <title>Case Status Lookup</title> </sp_page> </record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: Case Submission Widget
sys_update_xml.payload: per the agent prompt's Phase 1 specification, matching the sibling pattern used by ./sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget.xml and ./sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget.xml. - Indentation: 4 spaces for record fields (matching sibling-file convention). - Element ordering: alphabetical within the record (matching sibling-file convention; the ServiceNow Update Set XML serializer emits fields in alphabetical order). - sys_scope text-node value: `x_casemgmt` (matching the actual scope record's ). - sys_package text-node value: `x_casemgmt` with source="x_casemgmt" attribute (matching the sys_app record's ). - Deterministic 32-char lowercase hex sys_id: computed as MD5("sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_submission_widget") = b40f252d17be5fa3b3bb85cb79ca8d2f. Re-generation of this XML is byte-identical (no random UUIDs). - Audit fields: admin and 2025-01-01 00:00:00 for stable repeatable timestamps (the same date is used by EVERY sibling record in this scoped app for repeatable XML re-generation). - No smart quotes, no leading/trailing whitespace, no BOM. Strict XML conformance for ServiceNow Update Set re-import preview (AAP Section 0.7.3 validation gate 7). SOURCE-SIDE SEMANTIC REFERENCE (read-only context, NOT byte-for-byte ported per AAP Section 0.5.1): acm-services/acm-service-portal-gateway/src/main/java/com/armedia/acm/ portalgateway/service/DefaultPluggablePortalRequestService.java line 60-65 - submitRequest(...) implementation; resolves provider by request type and forwards. Returns a PortalResponse containing the confirmation identifier returned by the downstream provider (in ArkCase: a request id; in this ServiceNow target: the auto-generated case number). acm-standard-applications/arkcase/src/main/webapp/resources/modules/ cases/services/case-info.client.service.js line 13-42 - Case.InfoService factory using $resource against 'api/latest/plugin' with a save() method that POSTs to 'api/latest/plugin/casefile'. ArkCase's PortalRequestService accepted a typed PortalRequest DTO via Spring service injection; the request flowed through Spring MVC, the service implementation looked up a provider by request type, and the provider in turn delegated to a per-type bean for persistence. The ServiceNow target collapses this multi-hop delegation to: widget -> $http.post -> scripted REST -> Script Include -> GlideRecord. Three hops, not five. The semantic intent is preserved (anonymous submission -> confirmation with identifier) while the technical surface is restructured to fit ServiceNow's REST + Service Portal widget conventions. ArkCase's Angular service uses `$resource` (a higher-level wrapper around $http for RESTful resources). The ServiceNow target uses `$http.post()` directly because (a) Service Portal widget controllers do not load `ngResource` by default, and (b) the submit is a single POST, not a CRUD resource that warrants $resource's overhead. CROSS-REFERENCES (AAP Section 0.5.2 dependency ordering): THIS RECORD MUST LOAD AFTER: - ../../app/sys_scope/x_casemgmt.xml (scope record; cross-reference resolves against this record's ) - ../../app/sys_app/x_casemgmt_case_management.xml (scoped application record; cross-reference resolves against this record's ) - ../rest/sys_ws_definition_x_casemgmt_case_submit.xml (the scripted REST API namespace this widget calls) - ../rest/sys_ws_operation_x_casemgmt_case_submit_post.xml (the POST operation handler this widget invokes) - ../../script_includes/x_casemgmt_CasePortalService.xml (transitively, called by the REST operation; the submitCase() method enforces the server-side field whitelist and inserts the x_casemgmt_case row in Draft status) - ./sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget.xml (this widget embeds the confirmation widget via $sp.getWidget() by stable widget ID, so the confirmation widget must be present at import time for the embedded reference to resolve) THIS RECORD SHOULD LOAD BEFORE (but ServiceNow's two-pass import tolerates any order within the same Update Set, since references resolve by name): - ../pages/sp_page_x_casemgmt_case_submit.xml (the page hosts this widget by id `x_casemgmt_case_submission_widget`; the page's page_internal layout JSON references this widget by id, NOT by sys_id) VALIDATION-GATE MAPPING (AAP Section 0.7.3 - the seven validation gates): This widget supports Validation Gate 4 ("Portal - submission") by providing the user-facing UI for the case-submission capability. Without this widget: - Anonymous users would have no way to submit cases. - The portal page itself, the REST API definition, the REST API operation, the Script Include, and the confirmation widget would all be useless without the form to invoke them. - Validation Gate 4 would FAIL because there is no UI surface to test the "Case appears in internal list with Draft status and correct case number" requirement. Therefore THIS widget is the direct UI implementation behind Validation Gate 4; the AAP's "5 inputs" requirement is enforced by the template's form-field count and the controller's payload shape, and the AAP's "verbatim 'Your case has been submitted' message" requirement is enforced by the embedded confirmation widget that this widget renders on success. --> custom body. Contains: * c.data.confirmationWidget - the embedded * confirmation widget instance loaded via * $sp.getWidget(). The success callback * mutates c.data.confirmationWidget.options. * number to pass the auto-generated case * number to the embedded widget. * c.data.submitted - boolean; flipped to * true after a successful POST. Drives * the conditional swap from the form panel * to the embedded confirmation widget panel. * c.data.submittedNumber - string; mirrors * the case number for any consumers that * want it directly off c.data (defense-in- * depth, in case the embedded widget's * options object is replaced rather than * mutated by some future refactor). * * Exposed method (template-bound): * c.submit() - executes the case submission. Issues * POST /api/x_casemgmt/case_submit with the * 5-field whitelisted payload via $http.post(). * On success (HTTP 201 with non-empty number), * sets c.data.submitted = true and mutates the * embedded confirmation widget's options.number * to the returned case number. On any error * (HTTP 4xx/5xx, network, missing number), * sets c.errorMessage to a generic, non-leaking * message so the verbatim alert is rendered. * * Design rationale: * - controller-as syntax (`var c = this`) per AAP Section 0.5.1's * widget-authoring conventions and per the sibling lookup widget * at ./sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget.xml. * - $http (NOT fetch): Service Portal widgets run in AngularJS * 1.5.x context; $http is the canonical HTTP client. fetch() * would bypass AngularJS's digest cycle and require manual * $scope.$apply() handling. * - Strict 5-key payload (defense-in-depth): the POST body is * constructed by enumerating exactly the five whitelisted * fields, NOT by passing c.formData wholesale. Even if a future * refactor accidentally adds a sixth key to c.formData, the * POST body would still contain exactly the five whitelisted * fields. The server-side Script Include also drops any * non-whitelisted keys as a fallback, but the client-side * enumeration provides the first line of defense. * - Generic error message in template, NOT echoed from server: * the message "Submission failed. Please try again." is * hardcoded in this controller via direct assignment to * c.errorMessage. This controller deliberately does NOT pass * error.data.error or error.statusText to the template - the * server's error body is NEVER trusted by the client. This * matches the information-leakage hardening pattern used by * the sibling lookup widget. * - Identical handling of all HTTP errors: information-leakage * hardening. 400 (validation), 500 (server-error), 0 (network), * and "missing number on HTTP 200" all set the same generic * error. An attacker cannot distinguish "validation failed" * from "server error" from "network down" via the UX. * - c.submitting flag: prevents double-submit race conditions if * the user clicks the button twice quickly OR presses Enter * inside an input field while a request is in flight. The * ng-disabled binding on the button is the primary guard; * the early-return inside c.submit() is the defense-in-depth * fallback in case the disabled state is bypassed by a * keyboard event in an edge browser. * - String() coercion on response.data.number: ensures the * embedded widget's c.options.number is always a string, even * if the REST operation accidentally returns a number primitive. * Matches the embedded widget's own defensive coercion in * ./sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget.xml. */ var c = this; // Initialize the form state with empty values for each whitelisted field. // The whitelist (subject, type, description, requester_name, // requester_email) matches exactly the fields accepted by // /api/x_casemgmt/case_submit per AAP Section 0.4.4 and 0.7.4. c.formData = { subject: '', type: '', description: '', requester_name: '', requester_email: '' }; // Submission state flags. Initialized with safe defaults so the // template's ng-if and ng-disabled expressions evaluate cleanly // even before c.submit() runs. c.submitting = false; c.errorMessage = ''; /** * Submit the form data to the scripted REST endpoint at * POST /api/x_casemgmt/case_submit. * * Per AAP Section 0.4.4: posts EXACTLY the five whitelisted fields * (subject, type, description, requester_name, requester_email). * No additional fields are sent. * * Per AAP Section 0.4.4: on success, flips c.data.submitted to true * and passes the returned case number to the embedded confirmation * widget via c.data.confirmationWidget.options.number. The embedded * widget renders the verbatim message "Your case has been submitted" * plus the case number. * * Per AAP Section 0.7.2 (information-leakage hardening): on any * error (HTTP 4xx/5xx, network, missing number on HTTP 200), sets * c.errorMessage to the generic string "Submission failed. Please * try again." This function MUST NOT echo error.data.error, * error.statusText, or any server-supplied text. */ c.submit = function() { // Idempotent guard: prevent double-submit if the user clicks // the button or presses Enter while a request is in flight. // The button is also disabled in this case via ng-disabled, // but the guard provides defense-in-depth in case the disabled // state is bypassed by an edge browser's keyboard handling. if (c.submitting) { return; } // Reset state at the start of every submit. The error message // and submitting flag drive mutually-exclusive UI affordances. c.submitting = true; c.errorMessage = ''; // POST to the scripted REST endpoint. The payload is built by // enumerating EXACTLY the five whitelisted fields - NOT by // passing c.formData wholesale - so any future expansion of // c.formData does not accidentally widen the payload surface. // This is defense-in-depth alongside the server-side whitelist // enforced by x_casemgmt.CasePortalService.submitCase(). $http.post('/api/x_casemgmt/case_submit', { subject: c.formData.subject, type: c.formData.type, description: c.formData.description, requester_name: c.formData.requester_name, requester_email: c.formData.requester_email }).then(function(response) { // Success path (HTTP 2xx). The server's REST operation // returns { number, message } per its contract. c.submitting = false; if (response && response.data && response.data.number) { // Pass the returned case number to the embedded // confirmation widget by mutating its options.number. // The embedded widget's controller assigns // c.options = $scope.options; // so this mutation propagates reactively through // Angular's two-way binding. if (c.data.confirmationWidget && c.data.confirmationWidget.options) { c.data.confirmationWidget.options.number = String(response.data.number); } // Mirror the case number on c.data for any consumer that // wants it directly. Defense-in-depth in case the // embedded widget's options object is replaced rather // than mutated by some future refactor. c.data.submittedNumber = String(response.data.number); // Flip the submitted flag - the template swaps from // the form panel to the embedded confirmation widget. c.data.submitted = true; } else { // Defensive: HTTP 200 but no case number in the body. // This should never happen if the REST operation // conforms to its contract (it always returns either // { number, message } on success or { error } on // failure), but the guard prevents a confusing UX // where the form vanishes without a confirmation. c.errorMessage = 'Submission failed. Please try again.'; } }, function(error) { // Error path (HTTP 4xx, 5xx, or network failure / 0). // ALL error types are treated identically per the // information-leakage hardening discussed in the file // header comment. The generic error message is hardcoded // here in the controller; this function MUST NOT echo // error.data.error, error.statusText, or any server- // supplied content. c.submitting = false; c.errorMessage = 'Submission failed. Please try again.'; }); }; } ]]> c sp_instance Custom unauthenticated Service Portal widget for case submission. Renders a 5-field form (subject, type, description, requester_name, requester_email) and POSTs to /api/x_casemgmt/case_submit on Submit. On success, embeds the confirmation widget (sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget) via $sp.getWidget() and the <sp-widget> directive, passing the auto-generated case number through c.data.confirmationWidget.options.number so the embedded widget renders the verbatim message 'Your case has been submitted' plus the case number per AAP Section 0.4.4. On any HTTP error or missing-number response, displays a generic 'Submission failed. Please try again.' alert (no server diagnostics leaked). Embedded by sp_page_x_casemgmt_case_submit. The 5-field whitelist is enforced at three layers (form inputs, payload construction, and Script Include) for defense-in-depth. Replaces ArkCase's acm-service-portal-gateway DefaultPluggablePortalRequestService.submitRequest pattern. No client-side or server-side database operations - all writes flow through the scripted REST endpoint. Per AAP Sections 0.4.3, 0.4.4, 0.5.1, and 0.7.4 (verbatim 'Your case has been submitted' message lives in the embedded confirmation widget) and Validation Gate 4 (Portal - submission). false x_casemgmt_case_submission_widget false Case Submission Widget [] false true false sp_widget admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 b40f252d17be5fa3b3bb85cb79ca8d2f 0 Case Submission Widget x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_submission_widget admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00

sys_update_xml.name: Case Lookup Widget
sys_update_xml.payload: per the agent prompt's Phase 1 specification, matching the sibling pattern used by ../sp_portal_x_casemgmt_case_portal.xml and ./sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget.xml. - Indentation: 4 spaces for record fields (matching sibling-file convention). - Element ordering: alphabetical within the record (matching sibling-file convention; the ServiceNow Update Set XML serializer emits fields in alphabetical order). - sys_scope text-node value: `x_casemgmt` (matching the actual scope record's ). - sys_package text-node value: `x_casemgmt` with source="x_casemgmt" attribute (matching the sys_app record's ). - Deterministic 32-char lowercase hex sys_id: computed as MD5("sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget") = e992deb0bd1657f36a38f5af6a46aeb2. Re-generation of this XML is byte-identical (no random UUIDs). - Audit fields: admin and 2025-01-01 00:00:00 for stable repeatable timestamps (the same date is used by EVERY sibling record in this scoped app for repeatable XML re-generation). - No smart quotes, no leading/trailing whitespace, no BOM. Strict XML conformance for ServiceNow Update Set re-import preview (AAP Section 0.7.3 validation gate 7). SOURCE-SIDE SEMANTIC REFERENCE (read-only context, NOT byte-for-byte ported per AAP Section 0.5.1): acm-plugins/acm-default-plugins/acm-case-file-plugin/src/main/java/com/ armedia/acm/plugins/casefile/service/GetCaseByNumberService.java line 38 - CaseFile getCaseByNumber(String caseNumber); acm-plugins/acm-default-plugins/acm-case-file-plugin/src/main/java/com/ armedia/acm/plugins/casefile/service/GetCaseByNumberServiceImpl.java line 47-50 - public CaseFile getCaseByNumber(String caseNumber) { return getCaseFileDao().findByCaseNumber(caseNumber); } acm-standard-applications/arkcase/src/main/webapp/resources/modules/ cases/services/case-lookup.client.service.js line 13-30 - Case.LookupService factory using $resource against 'api/latest/plugin' ArkCase's JPA-DAO-backed lookup returns the FULL CaseFile entity with all internal fields (assigned_*, description, closed_date, requester_*, etc.). The ServiceNow target deliberately whitelists the response to ONLY status/subject/opened_date per AAP Section 0.4.4. The semantic intent is preserved (lookup by case number -> status); the technical surface is restructured to fit ServiceNow's REST + Service Portal widget conventions and adds the privacy whitelist that ArkCase did not have. ArkCase's AngularJS service uses `$resource` (a higher-level wrapper around $http for RESTful resources). The ServiceNow target uses `$http.get()` directly because (a) Service Portal widget controllers do not load `ngResource` by default, and (b) the lookup is a single GET, not a CRUD resource that warrants $resource's overhead. CROSS-REFERENCES (AAP Section 0.5.2 dependency ordering): THIS RECORD MUST LOAD AFTER: - ../../app/sys_scope/x_casemgmt.xml (scope record; cross-reference resolves against this record's ) - ../../app/sys_app/x_casemgmt_case_management.xml (scoped application record; cross-reference resolves against this record's ) - ../rest/sys_ws_definition_x_casemgmt_case_status_lookup.xml (the scripted REST API namespace this widget calls) - ../rest/sys_ws_operation_x_casemgmt_case_status_lookup_get.xml (the GET operation handler this widget invokes) - ../../script_includes/x_casemgmt_CasePortalService.xml (transitively, called by the REST operation; the lookupCase() method enforces the server-side field whitelist) THIS RECORD SHOULD LOAD BEFORE (but ServiceNow's two-pass import tolerates any order within the same Update Set, since references resolve by name): - ../pages/sp_page_x_casemgmt_case_status.xml (the page hosts this widget by id `x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget`; the page's page_internal layout JSON references this widget by id, NOT by sys_id) VALIDATION-GATE MAPPING (AAP Section 0.7.3 - the seven validation gates): This widget supports Validation Gate 5 ("Portal - lookup") by providing the user-facing UI for the case-status-lookup capability. Without this widget: - Anonymous users would have no way to look up case status. - The portal page itself, the REST API definition, the REST API operation, and the Script Include would all be useless without the UI to invoke them. - Validation Gate 5 would FAIL because there is no UI surface to test the "verbatim 'No case found with that number.' message" requirement. Therefore THIS widget is the direct UI implementation behind Validation Gate 5; the AAP's "verbatim text" requirement is enforced by the template's not-found panel. --> custom * via $http.get(). On success (HTTP 200), sets * c.result with the strict 3-field whitelist. On * any error (HTTP 4xx/5xx, network), sets * c.notFound = true so the verbatim message is * rendered. * * Design rationale: * - controller-as syntax (`var c = this`) per AAP Section 0.5.1's * widget-authoring conventions and per the sibling confirmation * widget at ./sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget.xml. * - $http (NOT fetch): Service Portal widgets run in AngularJS * 1.5.x context; $http is the canonical HTTP client. fetch() * would bypass AngularJS's digest cycle and require manual * $scope.$apply() handling. * - encodeURIComponent on the case number: defensive URL escaping. * Even though normal case numbers (CASE0000001) contain no * special characters, defensive encoding prevents URL-injection * attacks if a malicious user submits a crafted string. * - Strict 3-field whitelist on response.data: defense-in-depth. * Even if the server's response somehow contained additional * fields (e.g., due to a future refactor of the Script Include), * this client controller extracts ONLY status/subject/opened_date * so no internal data leaks to the template. * - Verbatim error message in template, NOT echoed from server: * the message "No case found with that number." (AAP Section * 0.7.4) is hardcoded in the template via ng-if="c.notFound". * This controller deliberately does NOT pass error.data.error * or error.statusText to the template - the server's error * body is NEVER trusted by the client. * - Identical handling of all HTTP errors: information-leakage * hardening. 404 (not-found), 500 (server-error), 400 (bad- * request), and 0 (network-error) all set c.notFound = true. * An attacker cannot distinguish "case not found" from "API * down" from any other failure mode. * - c.loading flag: prevents double-lookup race conditions if * the user clicks the button twice quickly, AND drives the * "Looking up..." button text for clear UX feedback. */ var c = this; // Lookup state. Initialize with safe defaults so the template's // ng-if expressions evaluate cleanly even before c.lookup() runs. c.caseNumber = ''; c.result = null; c.notFound = false; c.loading = false; /** * Look up the status of a case by its number via the scripted REST * endpoint at /api/x_casemgmt/case_status_lookup. * * Per AAP Section 0.4.4: returns ONLY status, subject, opened_date * via strict 3-field whitelist on the response. * * Per AAP Section 0.7.4: on no-match (HTTP 404 or any other error), * the verbatim text "No case found with that number." is rendered * by the template. This function MUST NOT echo error.data.error or * any server-supplied text. */ c.lookup = function() { // Idempotent guard: prevent double-lookup if the user clicks // the button or presses Enter while a request is in flight. if (c.loading) { return; } // Empty-input guard: do not issue a request if the case number // is empty or whitespace-only. The button is also disabled in // this case via ng-disabled, but the guard provides defense- // in-depth in case the disabled state is bypassed (e.g., by // pressing Enter in some edge browsers). if (!c.caseNumber || String(c.caseNumber).trim() === '') { return; } // Reset state at the start of every lookup. The result panel // and the not-found panel are mutually exclusive: only one of // c.result and c.notFound can be truthy at any time. c.loading = true; c.result = null; c.notFound = false; // Build the URL with defensive URL-encoding of the case number. // The server-side Script Include x_casemgmt.CasePortalService. // lookupCase() trims whitespace and queries by the case 'number' // field. encodeURIComponent() handles any whitespace, special // characters, or potentially malicious input safely. var url = '/api/x_casemgmt/case_status_lookup?number=' + encodeURIComponent(String(c.caseNumber).trim()); $http.get(url).then(function(response) { // Success path (HTTP 200). The server's REST operation // returns { status, subject, opened_date } per its contract. c.loading = false; if (response && response.data) { // Strict 3-field whitelist (defense-in-depth). Extract // ONLY status, subject, opened_date from the response // body. Even if the server were misconfigured to return // additional fields, this widget would not display them. // String() coercion handles undefined/null gracefully - // the template renders an empty cell rather than the // literal text "undefined" or "null". c.result = { status: String(response.data.status || ''), subject: String(response.data.subject || ''), opened_date: String(response.data.opened_date || '') }; c.notFound = false; } else { // Defensive: empty response body but HTTP 200. Treat as // not-found rather than rendering an empty result panel. // This should not occur in practice (the server always // returns a body on 200) but guards against unexpected // upstream behavior. c.result = null; c.notFound = true; } }, function(error) { // Error path (HTTP 4xx, 5xx, or network failure / 0). // ALL error types are treated identically per the // information-leakage hardening discussed in the file // header comment. The verbatim text "No case found with // that number." (AAP Section 0.7.4) is rendered by the // template; this function MUST NOT echo error.data.error, // error.statusText, or any server-supplied content. c.loading = false; c.result = null; c.notFound = true; }); }; } ]]> c sp_instance Custom unauthenticated Service Portal widget for case status lookup. Renders a single case-number input field plus a Look Up Status button, GETs /api/x_casemgmt/case_status_lookup, and displays ONLY the whitelisted fields { status, subject, opened_date } on match or the verbatim text 'No case found with that number.' on no-match per AAP Section 0.7.4. Embedded by sp_page_x_casemgmt_case_status. No internal fields (assigned_group, assigned_agent, description, closed_date, requester_name, requester_email, priority, type, pending_reason, audit fields) are exposed. All HTTP errors (404, 500, 400, network) render the same verbatim message for information-leakage hardening. Replaces ArkCase's GetCaseByNumberServiceImpl.getCaseByNumber() and PortalRequestService.getRequestStatus() Spring services. No client-side or server-side database operations - all data flows through the scripted REST endpoint. Per AAP Sections 0.4.3, 0.4.4, 0.5.1, and 0.7.4 (verbatim no-case-found message) and Validation Gate 5 (Portal - lookup). false x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget false Case Lookup Widget [] false true false sp_widget admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 e992deb0bd1657f36a38f5af6a46aeb2 0 Case Lookup Widget x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00

sys_update_xml.payload: per the agent prompt's Phase 1 specification, matching the sibling pattern used by ../sp_portal_x_casemgmt_case_portal.xml. - Indentation: 4 spaces for record fields (matching sibling-file convention). - Element ordering: alphabetical within the record (matching sibling-file convention). - sys_scope text-node value: `x_casemgmt` (matching the actual scope record's ). - sys_package text-node value: `x_casemgmt` with source="x_casemgmt" attribute (matching the sys_app record's ). - Deterministic 32-char lowercase hex sys_id: computed as MD5("sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget") = a198a7f3386d6b0b146451dcfa3c2126. Re-generation of this XML is byte-identical (no random UUIDs). - Audit fields: admin and 2025-01-01 00:00:00 for stable repeatable timestamps (the same date is used by EVERY sibling record in this scoped app for repeatable XML re-generation). - No smart quotes, no leading/trailing whitespace, no BOM. Strict XML conformance for ServiceNow Update Set re-import preview (AAP Section 0.7.3 validation gate 7). SOURCE-SIDE SEMANTIC REFERENCE (read-only context, NOT byte-for-byte ported per AAP Section 0.5.1): acm-services/acm-service-portal-gateway/src/main/java/com/armedia/acm/ portalgateway/service/DefaultPluggablePortalRequestService.java line 60-65 - submitRequest(...) implementation; resolves provider by request type and forwards. Returns a PortalResponse containing the confirmation identifier returned by the downstream provider (in ArkCase: a request id; in this ServiceNow target: the auto-generated case number). ArkCase's PortalResponse DTO carries the confirmation identifier returned by submitRequest(...). The ServiceNow target's confirmation widget consumes the equivalent identifier (the case number) and renders it. The semantic intent is preserved (display submission confirmation); the technology stack is restructured to fit ServiceNow's UI Builder/Service Portal conventions. CROSS-REFERENCES (AAP Section 0.5.2 dependency ordering): THIS RECORD MUST LOAD AFTER: - ../../app/sys_scope/x_casemgmt.xml (scope record; cross-reference resolves against this record's ) - ../../app/sys_app/x_casemgmt_case_management.xml (scoped application record; cross-reference resolves against this record's ) THIS RECORD SHOULD LOAD BEFORE (but ServiceNow's two-pass import tolerates any order within the same Update Set, since references resolve by name): - ./sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_submission_widget.xml (the submission widget embeds this widget via $sp.getWidget('x_casemgmt_case_ confirmation_widget', { number: ... })) - ../pages/sp_page_x_casemgmt_case_submit.xml (the page hosts the submission widget which transitively embeds this widget) VALIDATION-GATE MAPPING (AAP Section 0.7.3 - the seven validation gates): This widget supports Validation Gate 4 ("Portal - submission") by providing the success-confirmation UI that the submission widget swaps in after a successful POST. Without this widget, the submission user experience would have no terminal success state. --> custom c sp_instance Custom display-only Service Portal widget that shows the verbatim text 'Your case has been submitted' plus the auto-generated case number after a successful case submission. Embedded by sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_submission_widget via $sp.getWidget(); receives the case number via the 'number' widget option (exposed to the template as c.options.number). Per AAP Section 0.4.4 (verbatim 'Your case has been submitted' message) and Validation Gate 4 (Portal - submission). No client-side AJAX, no server-side database operations - pure display component. false x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget false Case Confirmation Widget [{"name":"number","label":"Case Number","section":"Presentation","type":"string","default_value":""}] false true false sp_widget admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 a198a7f3386d6b0b146451dcfa3c2126 0 Case Confirmation Widget x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00

sys_update_xml.name: Case Submit
sys_update_xml.payload: <record_update table="sys_ws_definition"> <sys_ws_definition action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <active>true</active> <api_id>case_submit</api_id> <consumes/> <consumes_customized>false</consumes_customized> <description>Scripted REST API definition for the Case Submit endpoint. Provides anonymously-accessible POST /api/x_casemgmt/case_submit for the unauthenticated Experience Portal submission widget. The actual HTTP-method handler lives in the paired sys_ws_operation record (sys_ws_operation_x_casemgmt_case_submit_post.xml). The handler delegates field whitelisting and persistence to the x_casemgmt.CasePortalService Script Include. Replaces ArkCase's acm-service-portal-gateway anonymous-submission pattern (DefaultPluggablePortalRequestService.submitRequest). Per AAP Sections 0.4.3, 0.4.4, 0.5.1.</description> <enforce_acl/> <name>Case Submit</name> <namespace>x_casemgmt</namespace> <produces/> <produces_customized>false</produces_customized> <requires_acl_authorization>false</requires_acl_authorization> <requires_authentication>false</requires_authentication> <requires_snc_internal_role>false</requires_snc_internal_role> <sys_class_name>sys_ws_definition</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>ef9090a5b03f1a36c248ce65065a1b74</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Case Submit</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>sys_ws_definition_x_casemgmt_case_submit</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> </sys_ws_definition> </record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: Case Status Lookup
sys_update_xml.payload: <record_update table="sys_ws_definition"> <sys_ws_definition action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <active>true</active> <api_id>case_status_lookup</api_id> <consumes/> <consumes_customized>false</consumes_customized> <description>Scripted REST API definition for the Case Status Lookup endpoint. Provides anonymously-accessible GET /api/x_casemgmt/case_status_lookup for the unauthenticated Experience Portal lookup widget (../widgets/sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_lookup_widget.xml). The HTTP-method handler lives in the paired sys_ws_operation record (sys_ws_operation_x_casemgmt_case_status_lookup_get.xml). The handler delegates the lookup and field-whitelist enforcement to the x_casemgmt.CasePortalService.lookupCase() Script Include method. Returns ONLY status, subject, opened_date (no internal fields) per AAP Sections 0.4.4 and 0.7.4. Returns the verbatim text 'No case found with that number.' on no-match. Replaces ArkCase's GetCaseByNumberService anonymous-status-lookup pattern. Anonymous access is enabled by setting requires_authentication=false, requires_acl_authorization=false, and requires_snc_internal_role=false on this definition record (and on the paired operation record); the privacy perimeter is the field whitelist in the Script Include, not table-level ACLs.</description> <enforce_acl/> <name>Case Status Lookup</name> <namespace>x_casemgmt</namespace> <produces/> <produces_customized>false</produces_customized> <requires_acl_authorization>false</requires_acl_authorization> <requires_authentication>false</requires_authentication> <requires_snc_internal_role>false</requires_snc_internal_role> <sys_class_name>sys_ws_definition</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>b04176032af365e6b8770ba3e6089b58</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Case Status Lookup</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>sys_ws_definition_x_casemgmt_case_status_lookup</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> </sys_ws_definition> </record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: Case Submit POST
sys_update_xml.payload:
<record_update table="sys_ws_operation">
    <sys_ws_operation action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE">
        <active>true</active>
        <consumes/>
        <consumes_customized>false</consumes_customized>
        <default_operation_uri>/api/x_casemgmt/case_submit</default_operation_uri>
        <description>Anonymous POST handler for case submission.</description>
        <enforce_acl/>
        <http_method>POST</http_method>
        <name>Case Submit POST</name>
        <operation_script><![CDATA[(function process(/*RESTAPIRequest*/ request, /*RESTAPIResponse*/ response) {
    try {
        var body = request.body && request.body.data ? request.body.data : {};
        var portalSvc = new x_casemgmt.CasePortalService();
        var result = portalSvc.submitCase(body);
        response.setStatus(201);
        response.setBody({ result: result });
    } catch (e) {
        response.setStatus(400);
        response.setBody({ error: { message: (e && e.message) || 'Submission failed.' } });
    }
})(request, response);
]]></operation_script>
        <operation_uri>/api/x_casemgmt/case_submit</operation_uri>
        <produces/>
        <produces_customized>false</produces_customized>
        <relative_path>/</relative_path>
        <request_example/>
        <requires_acl_authorization>false</requires_acl_authorization>
        <requires_authentication>false</requires_authentication>
        <requires_snc_internal_role>false</requires_snc_internal_role>
        <short_description>Anonymous POST handler for case submission.</short_description>
        <sys_class_name>sys_ws_operation</sys_class_name>
        <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by>
        <sys_created_on>2026-04-30 12:00:00</sys_created_on>
        <sys_id>886ad7128907a6351ea04b210c27029e</sys_id>
        <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count>
        <sys_name>Case Submit POST</sys_name>
        <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package>
        <sys_policy/>
        <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope>
        <sys_update_name>sys_ws_operation_x_casemgmt_case_submit_post</sys_update_name>
        <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by>
        <sys_updated_on>2026-04-30 12:00:00</sys_updated_on>
