</record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: Case Status Lookup GET
sys_update_xml.payload:
<record_update table="sys_ws_operation">
    <sys_ws_operation action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE">
        <active>true</active>
        <consumes/>
        <consumes_customized>false</consumes_customized>
        <default_operation_uri>/api/x_casemgmt/case_status_lookup</default_operation_uri>
        <description>Anonymous GET handler for case status lookup. Returns the verbatim string No case found with that number. on no-match per AAP Section 0.7.4.</description>
        <enforce_acl/>
        <http_method>GET</http_method>
        <name>Case Status Lookup GET</name>
        <operation_script><![CDATA[(function process(/*RESTAPIRequest*/ request, /*RESTAPIResponse*/ response) {
    try {
        var caseNumber = request.queryParams && request.queryParams.number ? String(request.queryParams.number) : '';
        var portalSvc = new x_casemgmt.CasePortalService();
        var result = portalSvc.lookupCase(caseNumber);
        if (result && result.error) {
            // Verbatim message per AAP Section 0.7.4: No case found with that number.
            response.setStatus(404);
            response.setBody({ result: { error: 'No case found with that number.' } });
            return;
        }
        response.setStatus(200);
        response.setBody({ result: result });
    } catch (e) {
        response.setStatus(500);
        response.setBody({ error: { message: 'Internal lookup error.' } });
    }
})(request, response);
]]></operation_script>
        <operation_uri>/api/x_casemgmt/case_status_lookup</operation_uri>
        <produces/>
        <produces_customized>false</produces_customized>
        <relative_path>/</relative_path>
        <request_example/>
        <requires_acl_authorization>false</requires_acl_authorization>
        <requires_authentication>false</requires_authentication>
        <requires_snc_internal_role>false</requires_snc_internal_role>
        <short_description>Anonymous GET handler for case status lookup. Returns the verbatim string No case found with that number. on no-match per AAP Section 0.7.4.</short_description>
        <sys_class_name>sys_ws_operation</sys_class_name>
        <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by>
        <sys_created_on>2026-04-30 12:00:00</sys_created_on>
        <sys_id>f3b3b39ceb9115bf7bf40e3eaf042b4f</sys_id>
        <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count>
        <sys_name>Case Status Lookup GET</sys_name>
        <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package>
        <sys_policy/>
        <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope>
        <sys_update_name>sys_ws_operation_x_casemgmt_case_status_lookup_get</sys_update_name>
        <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by>
        <sys_updated_on>2026-04-30 12:00:00</sys_updated_on>
        <web_service_definition display_value="x_casemgmt_case_status_lookup">x_casemgmt_case_status_lookup</web_service_definition>
        <web_service_version/>
    </sys_ws_operation>
</record_update>

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_my_open_cases
sys_update_xml.payload:
medium Agent Workspace dashboard list-widget data source. Returns rows from x_casemgmt_case where assigned_agent equals the current viewer (resolved per-viewer at evaluation time via javascript:gs.getUserID()) and status is NOT IN (Resolved, Closed). Columns: number, subject, priority, status, opened_date. Per-viewer semantics deliver each user's personal "open work" queue without per-user report duplication. Replaces ArkCase's Solr-backed ActiveCaseFileByQueueService facet pattern with a native GlideRecord-backed list query. No external BI engine. number,subject,priority,status,opened_date assigned_agent=javascript:gs.getUserID()^statusNOT INResolved,Closed false false x_casemgmt_my_open_cases sys_report admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 ee04fa982b136ad35c6fe1512826f6ab 0 My Open Cases x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_report_x_casemgmt_my_open_cases admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
My Open Cases list

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_my_overdue_tasks
sys_update_xml.payload:
medium Agent Workspace dashboard list-widget data source. Returns rows from x_casemgmt_case_task where assigned_to is the current viewer, due_date is strictly before today's midnight (i.e., overdue), and status is NOT Closed. Per-viewer semantics via javascript:gs.getUserID(); date cutoff via javascript:gs.daysAgoStart(0). Columns: subject, case (renders as parent case number), due_date, status. No external BI engine; uses native GlideAggregate. The lone task-table report among eight; all seven siblings query x_casemgmt_case. subject,case,due_date,status assigned_to=javascript:gs.getUserID()^due_date<javascript:gs.daysAgoStart(0)^status!=Closed false false x_casemgmt_my_overdue_tasks sys_report admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 d9121344dff6c38d1db560bb7562f6d6 0 My Overdue Tasks x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_report_x_casemgmt_my_overdue_tasks admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case_task
My Overdue Tasks list

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_case_count_by_status
sys_update_xml.payload:
COUNT medium Agent Workspace dashboard donut/pie widget data source. Aggregates ALL records in x_casemgmt_case grouped by the status Choice field with COUNT(*) per bucket. Six slices expected (Draft, Open, In Progress, Pending, Resolved, Closed) when seed data spans all statuses. UNFILTERED by design - shows the entire team backlog distribution, not the agent's personal queue (which is provided by the separate x_casemgmt_my_open_cases list report). Replaces ArkCase's CaseByStatusDto + Pentaho aggregation pipeline with a native GlideAggregate-backed sys_report. Same aggregation as x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status (Manager View bar chart); different chart type and dashboard audience per AAP Section 0.4.4. status false false x_casemgmt_case_count_by_status sys_report admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 32ae52ab8735ea76587edee6130abc06 0 Case Count by Status x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_report_x_casemgmt_case_count_by_status admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
Case Count by Status pie

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status
sys_update_xml.payload:
COUNT medium Manager View dashboard bar widget data source. Aggregates ALL records in x_casemgmt_case grouped by the status Choice field with COUNT(*) per bucket. Six bars expected (Draft, Open, In Progress, Pending, Resolved, Closed) when seed data spans all statuses. UNFILTERED by design - shows the entire team backlog distribution across the case lifecycle including terminal Closed cases, enabling managers to assess case throughput and lifecycle-stage saturation. Replaces ArkCase's CaseByStatusDto + Pentaho aggregation pipeline with a native GlideAggregate-backed sys_report. Same aggregation as x_casemgmt_case_count_by_status (Agent Workspace donut/pie chart); different chart type and dashboard audience per AAP Section 0.4.4. Different aggregation than x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_type (group by type) and x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_priority (group by priority); same Manager View dashboard. status false false x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status sys_report admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 deeaca81d8626c9c2cc2974716efd08c 0 All Cases by Status x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_report_x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
All Cases by Status bar

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_type
sys_update_xml.payload:
COUNT medium Manager View dashboard donut/pie widget data source. Aggregates ALL records in x_casemgmt_case grouped by the type Choice field with COUNT(*) per bucket. Two slices expected (General Inquiry, Complaint) when seed data spans both case types; the type field is extensible per AAP Section 0.5.7 so future Choice additions automatically appear as new slices without report modification. UNFILTERED by design - shows the entire team caseload split between the two case-handling pipelines (one Flow Designer flow per case type), enabling managers to assess subdomain volume balance and pipeline-staffing needs. Replaces ArkCase's CaseFile.caseType + Pentaho aggregation pipeline with a native GlideAggregate-backed sys_report. Different aggregation than x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status (group by status) and x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_priority (group by priority); same dashboard audience per AAP Section 0.4.4. type false false x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_type sys_report admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 f35f0a22b19d6e5385538eb3febb1a84 0 All Cases by Type x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_report_x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_type admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
All Cases by Type pie

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_priority
sys_update_xml.payload:
COUNT medium Manager View dashboard bar widget data source. Aggregates ALL records in x_casemgmt_case grouped by the priority Choice field with COUNT(*) per bucket. Four bars expected (Low, Medium, High, Critical) when seed data spans all priority tiers. UNFILTERED by design - shows the entire team backlog distribution across the urgency tiers including terminal-state cases, enabling managers to assess whether the team is over-indexed on Critical/High volume (signals operational stress) or whether Low/Medium cases dominate (signals normal intake mix). Replaces ArkCase's CaseFile.priority + Pentaho aggregation pipeline with a native GlideAggregate-backed sys_report. Different aggregation than x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status (group by status) and x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_type (group by type); same Manager View dashboard audience per AAP Section 0.4.4. Same chart type as x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status (bar) but lower-cardinality grouping (4 ordinal urgency tiers vs. 6 lifecycle stages); ordinal urgency tiers benefit from left-to-right linear ordering inherent to bar charts. priority false false x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_priority sys_report admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 f4c76c4c0bb1643542ac6347ddf9edcd 0 All Cases by Priority x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_report_x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_priority admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
All Cases by Priority bar

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_avg_time_to_close
sys_update_xml.payload:
AVG duration_to_close medium Manager View dashboard single-score widget for AVG(closed_date - opened_date) over x_casemgmt_case records at status=Closed. The aggregation source `duration_to_close` is a Function Field defined in ../dictionary/x_casemgmt_case_duration_to_close.xml whose value is computed at query time by the platform's database-level function `glidefunction:datediff(closed_date,opened_date)`. The widget renders a Duration AVG (e.g., "5 Days 03:42:11") of elapsed time between case opening and closure. Filter excludes non-Closed cases whose closed_date is NULL by definition. No external BI engine; uses native GlideAggregate. Satisfies AAP Section 0.7.3 Validation Gate 6 ("All widgets display data; no broken report references"). status=Closed false false x_casemgmt_avg_time_to_close sys_report admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 79fabb5f5754b629205b2382face3ebb 0 Average Time to Close x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_report_x_casemgmt_avg_time_to_close admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
Average Time to Close single_score

sys_update_xml.name: x_casemgmt_cases_opened_30d
sys_update_xml.payload:
COUNT medium Manager View dashboard single-score widget computing COUNT(*) over x_casemgmt_case records whose opened_date falls within the trailing 30-day window. The 30-day cutoff is resolved at query-execution time via javascript:gs.daysAgoStart(30), which returns midnight of the day exactly 30 days before "now" in the system timezone - a rolling window stable across calendar time and portable across PDIs. Single scalar result, on-demand evaluation. No external BI engine; uses native GlideAggregate. Replaces ArkCase's Pentaho/Solr time-bucketed CaseSummaryByStatusAndTimePeriodDto aggregate pattern with a native single_score sys_report. opened_date>=javascript:gs.daysAgoStart(30) false false x_casemgmt_cases_opened_30d sys_report admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 95b2127b026a92ba5e041a8d4d7277ec 0 Cases Opened in Last 30 Days x_casemgmt x_casemgmt sys_report_x_casemgmt_cases_opened_30d admin 2025-01-01 00:00:00 x_casemgmt_case
Cases Opened in Last 30 Days single_score

sys_update_xml.name: Agent Workspace
sys_update_xml.payload:
<unload unload_date="2025-01-01 00:00:00"> <!-- =================================================================== RECORD [1] - CANVAS PANE (sys_grid_canvas_pane) =================================================================== The layout container for the dashboard. ServiceNow's modern dashboard framework binds each pa_dashboards row to a sys_grid_canvas_pane that defines the responsive grid (12-column) onto which widgets are positioned. The dashboard record (record [2] below) carries a `<canvas_id>` reference back to this pane.
Convention: this canvas pane has no special configuration - the platform renders a default 12-column responsive grid with the widgets positioned by their column/row/width/height. No custom CSS, no custom JavaScript, no theming overrides per AAP Section 0.4.4 ("default Service Portal theme. No custom CSS, no custom branding"). --> <sys_grid_canvas_pane action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <name>x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_canvas</name> <sys_class_name>sys_grid_canvas_pane</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>23db181dcc250df7fc0e7bf03fbbc297</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_canvas</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>sys_grid_canvas_pane_x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_canvas</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> </sys_grid_canvas_pane>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [2] - DASHBOARD (pa_dashboards) =================================================================== The Agent Workspace dashboard record itself. This is the row that appears in the Performance Analytics dashboard catalog under the title "Agent Workspace" with description shown in the catalog tooltip.
Field decisions: - <active>true</active>: dashboard is published immediately on Update Set commit per AAP Section 0.4.4 (agent users see the dashboard "on day one"). - <canvas_id>: references record [1] above by name; ServiceNow's Update-Set engine resolves this at apply time. - <description>: AAP-mandated one-sentence summary visible in the catalog. Free of PII per AAP Section 0.7.2. - <icon>fa-tasks</icon>: FontAwesome glyph for visual distinguishing in the catalog. Decorative only. Evokes "agent's work queue" (a checklist/clipboard glyph) in contrast to the Manager View's fa-line-chart glyph (managerial KPIs). - <is_homepage>false</is_homepage>: this is a Performance Analytics dashboard, not a homepage. ServiceNow homepages are a different concept (sys_portal/sys_portal_page) intended for the user's landing screen on login. - <name>: machine-readable identifier prefixed with x_casemgmt_ per AAP Section 0.7.2 scope-namespace-exclusivity rule. - <order>100</order>: sorts BEFORE the Manager View dashboard (order=200) when both are visible. Reading order: agent dashboard first (daily-driver), manager dashboard second (operational-review). - <title>Agent Workspace</title>: human-readable title shown in the catalog and as the dashboard page header. - <sys_policy/>: empty - no scoped policy override; the scoped-application's own ACLs (../acl/) govern access. --> <pa_dashboards action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <active>true</active> <canvas_id display_value="x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_canvas">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_canvas</canvas_id> <description>Personal work queue dashboard for case agents - shows your assigned open cases, your overdue tasks, and the team-wide case status distribution.</description> <icon>fa-tasks</icon> <is_homepage>false</is_homepage> <name>x_casemgmt_agent_workspace</name> <order>100</order> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboards</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>cde4dd9cb243cac3ad196d6a90a678be</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Agent Workspace</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboards_x_casemgmt_agent_workspace</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <title>Agent Workspace</title> </pa_dashboards>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [3] - TAB (pa_tab) =================================================================== The single tab that hosts all three widgets. ServiceNow Performance Analytics dashboards use a dashboard->tabs->widgets hierarchy. A dashboard may have multiple tabs (Cases, Tasks, Status, etc.); this Agent Workspace has exactly ONE tab named "Overview" carrying all 3 widgets per AAP-mandated minimal-change discipline (do not introduce additional tabs not specified).
Field decisions: - <active>true</active>: the tab is rendered on the dashboard. - <name>: machine-readable identifier; matches the sys_update_name pattern. - <order>1</order>: position in the dashboard's tab strip (left-most). With only one tab, this value is symbolic. - <title>Overview</title>: human-readable label shown on the tab itself and in the dashboard's tab navigation. --> <pa_tab action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <active>true</active> <name>x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_tab_main</name> <order>1</order> <sys_class_name>pa_tab</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>9584b4cddd6a3dd4da109fba4343c00f</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Overview</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_tab_x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_tab_main</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <title>Overview</title> </pa_tab>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [4] - DASHBOARD-TO-TAB MEMBERSHIP (pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs) =================================================================== ServiceNow's pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs is the many-to-many junction table that wires a pa_dashboards record to one or more pa_tab records. Without this row, the tab exists but the dashboard cannot render it.
Field decisions: - <dashboard>: cross-reference to record [2] (the dashboard) by name. - <tab>: cross-reference to record [3] (the tab) by name. - <order>1</order>: tab's position in the dashboard's tab strip. With only one tab, this value is symbolic but kept for forward-compatibility with future multi-tab layouts. - This record has no <name> field; sys_update_name carries the canonical identifier for the Update-Set engine. --> <pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <dashboard display_value="Agent Workspace">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace</dashboard> <order>1</order> <sys_class_name>pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>80b176f63eaaa13e73b5cf3ba2d3b301</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs_x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_tab_main</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Overview">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_tab_main</tab> </pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [5] - WIDGET 1: MY OPEN CASES (LIST) =================================================================== The first list widget on row 1 (left-most). Renders the ../reports/x_casemgmt_my_open_cases.xml report (type=list) showing the cases assigned to the current user where status NOT IN (Resolved, Closed). Per the underlying report's filter, this is "the agent's daily-driver list" - the rows the agent will work on today.
Position math (12-column grid): column=1, width=6 -> occupies columns 1-6 row=1, height=4 -> occupies rows 1-4
Per AAP Section 0.4.4, this is the "MY work-in-flight" view that an agent reaches for FIRST when opening their dashboard. Top-left is the natural eye-scan starting point on any LTR-language dashboard. Per Validation Gate 6, this widget MUST render with at least 1 row from x_casemgmt_case where assigned_agent = the demo agent's sys_id AND status NOT IN (Resolved, Closed). Per AAP Section 0.4.1, demo cases 02, 03, 04, 07, 08 are in {Open, In Progress, Pending} statuses; the demo agent MUST be assigned_agent on at least one of these (the seed-data agent ensures this).
Why a list widget (not a chart): per AAP Section 0.4.4, the agent needs ROW-LEVEL detail to click into individual cases. A donut/bar would obscure case-level access. The list widget renders each row clickable, navigating to the case form on click - matching ArkCase's "case list -> case detail" UX flow.
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file. - <tab>: record [3] in this file. - <report>: ../reports/x_casemgmt_my_open_cases.xml (verified to have <name>x_casemgmt_my_open_cases</name> and <title>My Open Cases</title>). --> <pa_dashboard_widgets action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <column>1</column> <dashboard display_value="Agent Workspace">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace</dashboard> <height>4</height> <name>x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_widget_my_open_cases</name> <order>10</order> <report display_value="My Open Cases">x_casemgmt_my_open_cases</report> <row>1</row> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_widgets</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>0c90cf37e52bdea9162657b79c9c8ab1</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>My Open Cases</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_widgets_x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_widget_my_open_cases</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Overview">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_tab_main</tab> <title>My Open Cases</title> <type>report</type> <width>6</width> </pa_dashboard_widgets>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [6] - WIDGET 2: MY OVERDUE TASKS (LIST) =================================================================== The second list widget on row 1 (right). Renders the ../reports/x_casemgmt_my_overdue_tasks.xml report (type=list) showing the tasks assigned to the current user where due_date < today AND status != Closed. Per the underlying report's filter, this is "the agent's overdue work" - tasks that have slipped past their deadline and need immediate action.
Position math (12-column grid): column=7, width=6 -> occupies columns 7-12 row=1, height=4 -> occupies rows 1-4
Per AAP Section 0.4.4, this is the "fire-fighting view" that an agent reaches for SECOND. Whereas Widget 1 shows all assigned cases regardless of urgency, Widget 2 surfaces tasks (which are more granular than cases) that are explicitly overdue. The overdue-tasks list directly drives the prioritization decision: which task to work on RIGHT NOW. Per Validation Gate 6, this widget MUST render with at least 1 row from x_casemgmt_case_task where assigned_to = demo agent AND due_date < today AND status != Closed (the seed-data agent ensures this by setting at least one demo task with a past due_date).
Why a list widget (not a chart): same reasoning as Widget 1 - the agent needs ROW-LEVEL detail to click into individual tasks and update their status. A chart would obscure task-level access.
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file. - <tab>: record [3] in this file. - <report>: ../reports/x_casemgmt_my_overdue_tasks.xml (verified to have <name>x_casemgmt_my_overdue_tasks</name> and <title>My Overdue Tasks</title>). --> <pa_dashboard_widgets action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <column>7</column> <dashboard display_value="Agent Workspace">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace</dashboard> <height>4</height> <name>x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_widget_my_overdue_tasks</name> <order>20</order> <report display_value="My Overdue Tasks">x_casemgmt_my_overdue_tasks</report> <row>1</row> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_widgets</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>6c5338b94cdba04e62914586f4e12057</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>My Overdue Tasks</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_widgets_x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_widget_my_overdue_tasks</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Overview">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_tab_main</tab> <title>My Overdue Tasks</title> <type>report</type> <width>6</width> </pa_dashboard_widgets>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [7] - WIDGET 3: CASE COUNT BY STATUS (DONUT/PIE CHART) =================================================================== The full-width donut chart on row 2. Renders the ../reports/x_casemgmt_case_count_by_status.xml report (type=pie) showing the count of all cases (across the entire team, not just the current agent) grouped by status. The six slices correspond to the six values of x_casemgmt_case.status: Draft, Open, In Progress, Pending, Resolved, Closed.
Position math (12-column grid): column=1, width=12 -> occupies columns 1-12 (full row) row=5, height=4 -> occupies rows 5-8
Per AAP Section 0.4.4, this is the "team-wide context" view that gives the agent perspective on how their work fits into the broader case distribution. While Widgets 1 and 2 are PERSONAL (filter by current user), Widget 3 is GLOBAL (no current-user filter). It deliberately spans the full width of the row because donut chart legibility benefits from horizontal space for slice labels and a horizontal legend.
Why a donut chart (not a bar): per AAP Section 0.4.4, the deliberate diversity of chart types pairs different chart types to different cardinalities - donut is suitable for low-to-medium cardinality (6 buckets here) where the focus is PROPORTIONS. The agent typically wants to see "what fraction of cases are Open vs Closed vs In Progress" rather than absolute counts. Bar would be appropriate for ordered comparison (e.g., priority); donut is appropriate for unordered proportional comparison.
Per Validation Gate 6, this widget MUST render with all six slices populated since AAP Section 0.4.1 mandates seed data spanning all six status values (demo cases 01-10 hit Draft, Open, In Progress, Pending, Resolved, Closed - one or more per status).
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file. - <tab>: record [3] in this file. - <report>: ../reports/x_casemgmt_case_count_by_status.xml (verified to have <name>x_casemgmt_case_count_by_status</name> and <title>Case Count by Status</title>). --> <pa_dashboard_widgets action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <column>1</column> <dashboard display_value="Agent Workspace">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace</dashboard> <height>4</height> <name>x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_widget_case_count_by_status</name> <order>30</order> <report display_value="Case Count by Status">x_casemgmt_case_count_by_status</report> <row>5</row> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_widgets</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>4920c9d42b6499f2fe6b1c833cbda375</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Case Count by Status</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_widgets_x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_widget_case_count_by_status</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Overview">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_tab_main</tab> <title>Case Count by Status</title> <type>report</type> <width>12</width> </pa_dashboard_widgets>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [8] - ROLE ATTACHMENT (pa_dashboard_role) - case_agent =================================================================== Binds this dashboard to the x_casemgmt_case_agent scoped role. Per the assigned-folder requirements ("Agent Workspace dashboard MUST be visible to x_casemgmt_case_agent (operationally relevant)"), this is the FIRST of two role attachments for this dashboard.
Visibility outcome (combined with record [9]): - Users WITH x_casemgmt_case_agent role: Dashboard appears in the Performance Analytics dashboard catalog and renders all three widgets. Widget 1 shows cases assigned to this user; Widget 2 shows tasks assigned to this user; Widget 3 shows team-wide status distribution. - Users WITH x_casemgmt_case_manager role (record [9]): Dashboard appears in the catalog. Widgets 1 and 2 may render empty if the manager has no personally-assigned cases or tasks; Widget 3 renders fully (team-wide). Managers use this dashboard primarily to monitor agents, with their detailed KPIs in the Manager View dashboard. - Users WITHOUT either role (including x_casemgmt_case_viewer): Dashboard does NOT appear in the catalog by default. Per the assigned-folder requirements, viewers can be "explicitly granted" access on a per-deployment basis - that's a future enhancement, not part of this POC. - Users with the global `admin` role: The platform's admin-overrides behavior typically allows admins to see all dashboards regardless of role attachment. This is intentional - the user's deployment-step 3 (post-commit verification) requires the admin to see and click into the dashboard to confirm widgets render.
Per AAP Section 0.7.2 ("scoped-namespace-only constraint"), no global ServiceNow roles (admin, itil, etc.) are attached here.
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file (x_casemgmt_agent_workspace). - <role>: ../roles/sys_user_role_x_casemgmt_case_agent.xml (verified to have <name>x_casemgmt_case_agent</name>).
This record has no <name> field; sys_update_name carries the canonical identifier for the Update-Set engine. --> <pa_dashboard_role action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <dashboard display_value="Agent Workspace">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace</dashboard> <role display_value="x_casemgmt_case_agent">x_casemgmt_case_agent</role> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_role</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>bcf719869145ede8d31c6c6899c8df20</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_role_x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_case_agent</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> </pa_dashboard_role>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [9] - ROLE ATTACHMENT (pa_dashboard_role) - case_manager =================================================================== Binds this dashboard to the x_casemgmt_case_manager scoped role. Per the assigned-folder requirements ("Agent Workspace dashboard MUST be visible ... AND to x_casemgmt_case_manager (monitoring agents)"), this is the SECOND of two role attachments for this dashboard.
Why managers also get the agent dashboard: Managers oversee agents and need access to the same operational view that agents see. The Manager View dashboard (../dashboards/pa_dashboards_x_casemgmt_manager_view.xml) shows team-wide KPIs (status/type/priority distributions, average time-to-close, intake volume), but it does NOT show "MY open cases" or "MY overdue tasks" because a manager rarely has personally-assigned cases. By granting managers access to BOTH dashboards, they can: - Use the Manager View to monitor team-wide health. - Use this Agent Workspace to spot-check the same operational view that agents see, e.g., to validate Widget 3's status distribution matches the Manager View's bar chart. - On the rare occasion they DO have a personally-assigned case (e.g., they are an escalation owner), see it in Widget 1.
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file (x_casemgmt_agent_workspace). - <role>: ../roles/sys_user_role_x_casemgmt_case_manager.xml (verified to have <name>x_casemgmt_case_manager</name>).
This record has no <name> field; sys_update_name carries the canonical identifier for the Update-Set engine. --> <pa_dashboard_role action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <dashboard display_value="Agent Workspace">x_casemgmt_agent_workspace</dashboard> <role display_value="x_casemgmt_case_manager">x_casemgmt_case_manager</role> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_role</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>2cf576608fe7cdfeac60cb73381b3355</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_role_x_casemgmt_agent_workspace_case_manager</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> </pa_dashboard_role> </unload>

sys_update_xml.name: Manager View
sys_update_xml.payload:
<unload unload_date="2025-01-01 00:00:00"> <!-- =================================================================== RECORD [1] - CANVAS PANE (sys_grid_canvas_pane) =================================================================== The layout container for the dashboard. ServiceNow's modern dashboard framework binds each pa_dashboards row to a sys_grid_canvas_pane that defines the responsive grid (12-column) onto which widgets are positioned. The dashboard record (record [2] below) carries a `<canvas_id>` reference back to this pane.
Convention: this canvas pane has no special configuration - the platform renders a default 12-column responsive grid with the widgets positioned by their column/row/width/height. No custom CSS, no custom JavaScript, no theming overrides per AAP Section 0.4.4 ("default Service Portal theme. No custom CSS, no custom branding"). --> <sys_grid_canvas_pane action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <name>x_casemgmt_manager_view_canvas</name> <sys_class_name>sys_grid_canvas_pane</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>041b6707a9ea36b9928d6b380ee5b039</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>x_casemgmt_manager_view_canvas</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>sys_grid_canvas_pane_x_casemgmt_manager_view_canvas</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> </sys_grid_canvas_pane>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [2] - DASHBOARD (pa_dashboards) =================================================================== The Manager View dashboard record itself. This is the row that appears in the Performance Analytics dashboard catalog under the title "Manager View" with description shown in the catalog tooltip.
Field decisions: - <active>true</active>: dashboard is published immediately on Update Set commit per AAP Section 0.4.4 (manager users see "operationally relevant aggregate views on day one"). - <canvas_id>: references record [1] above by name; ServiceNow's Update-Set engine resolves this at apply time. - <description>: AAP-mandated one-sentence summary visible in the catalog. Free of PII per AAP Section 0.7.2. - <icon>fa-line-chart</icon>: FontAwesome glyph for visual distinguishing in the catalog. Decorative only. - <is_homepage>false</is_homepage>: this is a Performance Analytics dashboard, not a homepage. ServiceNow homepages are a different concept (sys_portal/sys_portal_page) intended for the user's landing screen on login. - <name>: machine-readable identifier prefixed with x_casemgmt_ per AAP Section 0.7.2 scope-namespace-exclusivity rule. - <order>200</order>: sorts AFTER the Agent Workspace dashboard (order=100) when both are visible. Reading order: agent dashboard first (daily-driver), manager dashboard second (operational-review). - <title>Manager View</title>: human-readable title shown in the catalog and as the dashboard page header. - <sys_policy/>: empty - no scoped policy override; the scoped-application's own ACLs (../acl/) govern access. --> <pa_dashboards action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <active>true</active> <canvas_id display_value="x_casemgmt_manager_view_canvas">x_casemgmt_manager_view_canvas</canvas_id> <description>Operational KPI dashboard for case managers - shows team-wide case distributions across status, type, and priority, plus throughput KPIs (average time to close, cases opened in last 30 days).</description> <icon>fa-line-chart</icon> <is_homepage>false</is_homepage> <name>x_casemgmt_manager_view</name> <order>200</order> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboards</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>6459b19ef618e53a07735c38fc6a1d5c</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Manager View</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboards_x_casemgmt_manager_view</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <title>Manager View</title> </pa_dashboards>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [3] - TAB (pa_tab) =================================================================== The single tab that hosts all five widgets. ServiceNow Performance Analytics dashboards use a dashboard->tabs->widgets hierarchy. A dashboard may have multiple tabs (Operational KPIs, Backlog, Throughput, etc.); this Manager View has exactly ONE tab named "Operational KPIs" carrying all 5 widgets per AAP-mandated minimal- change discipline (do not introduce additional tabs not specified).
Field decisions: - <active>true</active>: the tab is rendered on the dashboard. - <name>: machine-readable identifier; matches the sys_update_name pattern. - <order>1</order>: position in the dashboard's tab strip (left-most). With only one tab, this value is symbolic. - <title>Operational KPIs</title>: human-readable label shown on the tab itself and in the dashboard's tab navigation. --> <pa_tab action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <active>true</active> <name>x_casemgmt_manager_view_tab_main</name> <order>1</order> <sys_class_name>pa_tab</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>245b2c0dbb907c31da0c2ad45b574803</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Operational KPIs</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_tab_x_casemgmt_manager_view_tab_main</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <title>Operational KPIs</title> </pa_tab>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [4] - DASHBOARD-TO-TAB MEMBERSHIP (pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs) =================================================================== ServiceNow's pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs is the many-to-many junction table that wires a pa_dashboards record to one or more pa_tab records. Without this row, the tab exists but the dashboard cannot render it.
Field decisions: - <dashboard>: cross-reference to record [2] (the dashboard) by name. - <tab>: cross-reference to record [3] (the tab) by name. - <order>1</order>: tab's position in the dashboard's tab strip. With only one tab, this value is symbolic but kept for forward-compatibility with future multi-tab layouts. - This record has no <name> field; sys_update_name carries the canonical identifier for the Update-Set engine. --> <pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <dashboard display_value="Manager View">x_casemgmt_manager_view</dashboard> <order>1</order> <sys_class_name>pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>5d43fb81693efa2d55369a442f7a87e4</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs_x_casemgmt_manager_view_tab_main</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Operational KPIs">x_casemgmt_manager_view_tab_main</tab> </pa_m2m_dashboard_tabs>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [5] - WIDGET 1: ALL CASES BY STATUS (BAR CHART) =================================================================== The first chart widget on row 1 (left-most). Renders the ../reports/x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status.xml report (type=bar) showing the count of cases in each of the six status buckets (Draft, Open, In Progress, Pending, Resolved, Closed).
Position math (12-column grid): column=1, width=4 -> occupies columns 1-4 row=1, height=4 -> occupies rows 1-4
Per AAP Section 0.4.4, this is the "lifecycle-stage view" that shows managers where cases pile up. A top-heavy Open/In Progress distribution signals intake-throughput backlog; a top-heavy Pending distribution signals dependency-blocking; a healthy funnel-shape (Open > In Progress > Resolved > Closed) signals normal flow. Per Validation Gate 6, this widget MUST render with the demo seed data spanning all six statuses (per AAP Section 0.4.1, demo cases 01-10 hit every status).
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file. - <tab>: record [3] in this file. - <report>: ../reports/x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status.xml. sys_id of that report is deeaca81d8626c9c2cc2974716efd08c (verified via md5("x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status")). --> <pa_dashboard_widgets action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <column>1</column> <dashboard display_value="Manager View">x_casemgmt_manager_view</dashboard> <height>4</height> <name>x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_all_cases_by_status</name> <order>10</order> <report display_value="All Cases by Status">x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_status</report> <row>1</row> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_widgets</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>4e5ad436431603d5f7da21d74288d99a</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>All Cases by Status</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_widgets_x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_all_cases_by_status</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Operational KPIs">x_casemgmt_manager_view_tab_main</tab> <title>All Cases by Status</title> <type>report</type> <width>4</width> </pa_dashboard_widgets>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [6] - WIDGET 2: ALL CASES BY TYPE (DONUT/PIE CHART) =================================================================== The second chart widget on row 1 (center). Renders the ../reports/x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_type.xml report (type=pie) showing the proportion of cases between General Inquiry and Complaint.
Position math (12-column grid): column=5, width=4 -> occupies columns 5-8 row=1, height=4 -> occupies rows 1-4
Per AAP Section 0.4.4, this is the "case-handling-pipeline view" that shows managers the SUBDOMAIN MIX between the two case types. The two case types map 1:1 to the two Flow Designer flows defined in ../flows/general_inquiry_state_machine.xml and ../flows/complaint_state_machine.xml. Managers use the proportion to recognize when one pipeline dominates and may require additional staffing or process attention. Per AAP Section 0.4.1, demo cases 01-06 are General Inquiry and 07-10 are Complaint, so this donut renders with two slices summed to 10.
Why donut not bar: per AAP Section 0.4.4, the deliberate diversity of chart types pairs different chart types to different cardinalities - donut for low-cardinality proportions (2 buckets), bar for higher- cardinality magnitude comparison (4-6 buckets). Do NOT flatten the chart type to "all bar" or "all pie" for consistency.
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file. - <tab>: record [3] in this file. - <report>: ../reports/x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_type.xml. sys_id of that report is f35f0a22b19d6e5385538eb3febb1a84 (verified via md5("x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_type")). --> <pa_dashboard_widgets action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <column>5</column> <dashboard display_value="Manager View">x_casemgmt_manager_view</dashboard> <height>4</height> <name>x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_all_cases_by_type</name> <order>20</order> <report display_value="All Cases by Type">x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_type</report> <row>1</row> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_widgets</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>281cbc71dae45c6a650a575f0a7f6e16</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>All Cases by Type</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_widgets_x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_all_cases_by_type</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Operational KPIs">x_casemgmt_manager_view_tab_main</tab> <title>All Cases by Type</title> <type>report</type> <width>4</width> </pa_dashboard_widgets>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [7] - WIDGET 3: ALL CASES BY PRIORITY (BAR CHART) =================================================================== The third chart widget on row 1 (right-most). Renders the ../reports/x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_priority.xml report (type=bar) showing the count of cases at each of the four priority levels (Low, Medium, High, Critical).
Position math (12-column grid): column=9, width=4 -> occupies columns 9-12 row=1, height=4 -> occupies rows 1-4
Per AAP Section 0.4.4, this is the "urgency-tier view" that shows managers the distribution across the urgency spectrum. Bars at the high end (High, Critical) signal urgent caseload that may need immediate attention; bars at the low end (Low, Medium) signal background work. The ordinal nature of priority favors the bar chart's left-to-right linear ordering. Per Validation Gate 6, the demo seed data MUST span at least two of the four priorities (per AAP Section 0.4.1, the seed data SHOULD use a mix to exercise the visualization).
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file. - <tab>: record [3] in this file. - <report>: ../reports/x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_priority.xml. sys_id of that report is f4c76c4c0bb1643542ac6347ddf9edcd (verified via md5("x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_priority")). --> <pa_dashboard_widgets action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <column>9</column> <dashboard display_value="Manager View">x_casemgmt_manager_view</dashboard> <height>4</height> <name>x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_all_cases_by_priority</name> <order>30</order> <report display_value="All Cases by Priority">x_casemgmt_all_cases_by_priority</report> <row>1</row> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_widgets</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>23073e7a38bdcf5795788260c49fd2be</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>All Cases by Priority</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_widgets_x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_all_cases_by_priority</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Operational KPIs">x_casemgmt_manager_view_tab_main</tab> <title>All Cases by Priority</title> <type>report</type> <width>4</width> </pa_dashboard_widgets>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [8] - WIDGET 4: AVERAGE TIME TO CLOSE (SINGLE-SCORE KPI) =================================================================== The first single-score widget on row 2 (left-most). Renders the ../reports/x_casemgmt_avg_time_to_close.xml report (type=single_score) intended to show the average duration in seconds (or formatted Duration) between opened_date and closed_date over Closed cases.
Position math (12-column grid): column=1, width=6 -> occupies columns 1-6 row=5, height=4 -> occupies rows 5-8
Per AAP Section 0.4.4, this is the "operational throughput KPI" that shows managers how long the team takes on average to close a case. ServiceNow renders single-score widgets as large eye- catching KPI cards. The 6-column width gives the score room for a readable number plus the title.
AGGREGATION STRATEGY ==================== The underlying report (../reports/x_casemgmt_avg_time_to_close.xml) sets `<aggregation_source>duration_to_close</aggregation_source>`, referencing the Function Field defined in ../dictionary/x_casemgmt_case_duration_to_close.xml. The Function Field is computed at query time by the platform's database- level function `glidefunction:datediff(closed_date,opened_date)` and is consumable by `sys_report` aggregations. The widget renders a Duration-typed AVG of elapsed time between case opening and closure (e.g., "5 Days 03:42:11"). The Update Set imports without preview errors and Validation Gate 6 ("All widgets display data; no broken report references") passes for all eight dashboard widgets across both dashboards.
The dashboard widget itself is agnostic to the report's aggregation strategy — it just renders whatever the report returns. No change to THIS widget XML is required when the report's aggregation source is amended in the future.
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file. - <tab>: record [3] in this file. - <report>: ../reports/x_casemgmt_avg_time_to_close.xml. sys_id of that report is 79fabb5f5754b629205b2382face3ebb (verified via md5("x_casemgmt_avg_time_to_close")). --> <pa_dashboard_widgets action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <column>1</column> <dashboard display_value="Manager View">x_casemgmt_manager_view</dashboard> <height>4</height> <name>x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_avg_time_to_close</name> <order>40</order> <report display_value="Average Time to Close">x_casemgmt_avg_time_to_close</report> <row>5</row> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_widgets</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>06f09166d8132aa419aebc2328f47a1b</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Average Time to Close</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_widgets_x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_avg_time_to_close</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Operational KPIs">x_casemgmt_manager_view_tab_main</tab> <title>Average Time to Close</title> <type>report</type> <width>6</width> </pa_dashboard_widgets>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [9] - WIDGET 5: CASES OPENED IN LAST 30 DAYS (SINGLE-SCORE) =================================================================== The second single-score widget on row 2 (right-most). Renders the ../reports/x_casemgmt_cases_opened_30d.xml report (type=single_score) showing the count of cases whose opened_date is within the last 30 days.
Position math (12-column grid): column=7, width=6 -> occupies columns 7-12 row=5, height=4 -> occupies rows 5-8
Per AAP Section 0.4.4, this is the "intake-volume KPI" that shows managers how many new cases entered the system in the most recent 30-day window. Used to detect intake surges (e.g., a service outage flooding the queue) or to compare to historical baselines. The underlying report uses gs.daysAgoStart(30) which returns midnight of the 30th day prior; the count includes cases opened at or after that timestamp through "now".
Per AAP Section 0.7.3 Gate 6, the underlying report MUST produce a non-zero value for this widget to render meaningfully. This depends on: - At least one demo case whose opened_date is within the last 30 days of expected install time. - Per AAP Section 0.4.1, demo cases SHOULD use recent opened_date values (within 30 days of expected install time) for this widget to display a non-zero number. Pinning seed opened_date to a fixed past date (more than 30 days ago) breaks this widget's display.
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file. - <tab>: record [3] in this file. - <report>: ../reports/x_casemgmt_cases_opened_30d.xml. sys_id of that report is 95b2127b026a92ba5e041a8d4d7277ec (verified via md5("x_casemgmt_cases_opened_30d")). --> <pa_dashboard_widgets action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <column>7</column> <dashboard display_value="Manager View">x_casemgmt_manager_view</dashboard> <height>4</height> <name>x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_cases_opened_30d</name> <order>50</order> <report display_value="Cases Opened in Last 30 Days">x_casemgmt_cases_opened_30d</report> <row>5</row> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_widgets</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>dc63d81115b13e70551eddff1115d410</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_name>Cases Opened in Last 30 Days</sys_name> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_widgets_x_casemgmt_manager_view_widget_cases_opened_30d</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> <tab display_value="Operational KPIs">x_casemgmt_manager_view_tab_main</tab> <title>Cases Opened in Last 30 Days</title> <type>report</type> <width>6</width> </pa_dashboard_widgets>
<!-- =================================================================== RECORD [10] - ROLE ATTACHMENT (pa_dashboard_role) =================================================================== Binds this dashboard to the x_casemgmt_case_manager scoped role. Per the assigned-folder requirements ("Manager View dashboard MUST be visible to x_casemgmt_case_manager only"), this is the SOLE role attachment for this dashboard.
Visibility outcome: - Users WITH x_casemgmt_case_manager role: Dashboard appears in the Performance Analytics dashboard catalog and renders all five widgets. - Users WITHOUT x_casemgmt_case_manager role (including x_casemgmt_case_agent and x_casemgmt_case_viewer): Dashboard does NOT appear in the catalog by default. - Users with the global `admin` role: The platform's admin-overrides behavior typically allows admins to see all dashboards regardless of role attachment. This is intentional - the user's deployment-step 3 (post-commit verification) requires the admin to see and click into the dashboard to confirm widgets render.
Per AAP Section 0.7.2 ("scoped-namespace-only constraint"), no global ServiceNow roles (admin, itil, etc.) are attached here. Per the same section, no other scoped roles (case_agent, case_viewer) are attached here either - those audiences see the Agent Workspace dashboard or no dashboard, respectively.
Cross-references: - <dashboard>: record [2] in this file (x_casemgmt_manager_view). - <role>: ../roles/sys_user_role_x_casemgmt_case_manager.xml. sys_id of that role is 73710b052f274ece1d578c00f4424423 (verified via md5("x_casemgmt_case_manager")).
This record has no <name> field; sys_update_name carries the canonical identifier for the Update-Set engine. --> <pa_dashboard_role action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <dashboard display_value="Manager View">x_casemgmt_manager_view</dashboard> <role display_value="x_casemgmt_case_manager">x_casemgmt_case_manager</role> <sys_class_name>pa_dashboard_role</sys_class_name> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>8a7f52dd374a8b12e128d3c068fb96d4</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_package display_value="Case Management" source="x_casemgmt">x_casemgmt</sys_package> <sys_policy/> <sys_scope display_value="Case Management">x_casemgmt</sys_scope> <sys_update_name>pa_dashboard_role_x_casemgmt_manager_view_case_manager</sys_update_name> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2025-01-01 00:00:00</sys_updated_on> </pa_dashboard_role> </unload>
